en.tldr: Proposes methods to construct and evaluate Hypergraph of Text, introduces effort ratio metric, and shows simple TF-IDF baselines can match LLM-based methods.
en.motivation: Web pages are navigable due to hyperlinks, but manual hyperlinks cannot fully capture implicit semantic structures; a general method to make any collection navigable is needed.
en.method: Proposes several construction methods for Hypergraph of Text and a novel quantitative metric, effort ratio, to evaluate structural quality.
en.result: Experiments show that simple TF-IDF baselines can match LLM-based methods on the effort ratio metric.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to turn any text collection into a navigable hypergraph and provides a quantitative metric for evaluating its quality.

idea_spark.idea: Use lightweight methods like TF-IDF instead of expensive LLMs for fast navigation construction on large text collections.
idea_spark.risk: Simple baselines may fail to capture deep semantics, limiting navigation quality on complex corpora.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires exploration of more efficient and scalable text navigation structures without relying on large models.

papers.title: TopoMamba: Topology-Aware Scanning and Fusion for Segmenting Heterogeneous Medical Visual Media
papers.summary: Visual state-space models (SSMs) have shown strong potential for medical image segmentation, yet their effectiveness is often limited by two practical issues: axis-biased scan ordering weakens the modeling of oblique and curved structures, and naive multi-branch fusion tends to amplify redundant responses. We present TopoMamba, a topology-aware scan-and-fuse framework for segmenting heterogeneous medical visual media. The method combines a diagonal/anti-diagonal TopoA-Scan branch with the standard Cross-Scan branch to provide complementary structural priors, and introduces ScanCache, a device-aware caching mechanism that amortizes explicit scan-index construction across recurring resolutions. To fuse heterogeneous scan features efficiently, we further propose a lightweight HSIC Gate that regulates branch interaction using a dependence-aware scalar gating rule. We also instantiate a volumetric TopoMamba-3D for practical 3D clinical segmentation. Experiments on Synapse CT, ISIC 2017 dermoscopy, and CVC-ClinicDB endoscopy show that TopoMamba consistently improves segmentation quality over strong CNN, Transformer, and SSM baselines, with particularly clear gains on thin or curved targets such as the pancreas and gallbladder, while maintaining favorable deployment efficiency under dynamic input resolutions. These results suggest that topology-aware scan ordering and lightweight dependence-aware fusion form an effective and practical design for medical multimedia segmentation. The code will be made publicly available.

ai.tldr: 提出TopoMamba，一种拓扑感知的扫描与融合框架，用于分割异构医学视觉媒体，在多个数据集上优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有视觉状态空间模型在医学图像分割中存在轴偏扫描顺序削弱斜向和弯曲结构建模、以及朴素多分支融合放大冗余响应的问题。
ai.method: 结合对角/反对角TopoA-Scan分支与标准Cross-Scan分支提供互补结构先验；引入ScanCache缓存机制摊销显式扫描索引构建；提出轻量HSIC Gate通过依赖感知标量门控高效融合异构扫描特征。
ai.result: 在Synapse CT、ISIC 2017和CVC-ClinicDB上，TopoMamba一致优于CNN、Transformer和SSM基线，尤其在胰腺、胆囊等薄或弯曲目标上提升明显，且保持良好部署效率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过拓扑感知扫描和轻量依赖感知融合提升医学图像分割质量，尤其适用于细长或弯曲结构。

idea_spark.idea: 将拓扑感知扫描与注意力机制结合，进一步捕捉全局上下文依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 对角/反对角扫描可能增加计算开销，需在效率与精度间权衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受拓扑数据分析启发，将结构先验融入序列建模，为SSM在医学图像中的应用提供新思路。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过拓扑感知扫描和轻量依赖感知融合提升医学图像分割质量，尤其适用于细长或弯曲结构。

idea_spark.idea: 将拓扑感知扫描与注意力机制结合，进一步捕捉全局上下文依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 对角/反对角扫描可能增加计算开销，需在效率与精度间权衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受拓扑数据分析启发，将结构先验融入序列建模，为SSM在医学图像中的应用提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes TopoMamba, a topology-aware scan-and-fuse framework for segmenting heterogeneous medical visual media, outperforming existing methods on multiple datasets.
en.motivation: Existing visual state-space models for medical image segmentation suffer from axis-biased scan ordering that weakens modeling of oblique and curved structures, and naive multi-branch fusion that amplifies redundant responses.
en.method: Combines a diagonal/anti-diagonal TopoA-Scan branch with the standard Cross-Scan branch for complementary structural priors; introduces ScanCache to amortize explicit scan-index construction; proposes a lightweight HSIC Gate for dependence-aware scalar gating to efficiently fuse heterogeneous scan features.
en.result: On Synapse CT, ISIC 2017, and CVC-ClinicDB, TopoMamba consistently outperforms strong CNN, Transformer, and SSM baselines, with clear gains on thin or curved targets like pancreas and gallbladder, while maintaining favorable deployment efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how topology-aware scanning and lightweight dependence-aware fusion improve medical image segmentation, especially for thin or curved structures.

idea_spark.idea: Combine topology-aware scanning with attention mechanisms to further capture global context dependencies.
idea_spark.risk: Diagonal/anti-diagonal scanning may increase computational cost, requiring a trade-off between efficiency and accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by topological data analysis, integrating structural priors into sequence modeling offers a new direction for SSMs in medical imaging.

papers.title: Semantic Layers for Reliable LLM-Powered Data Analytics: A Paired Benchmark of Accuracy and Hallucination Across Three Frontier Models
papers.summary: LLMs deployed for natural-language querying of analytical databases suffer from two intertwined failures - incorrect answers and confident hallucinations - both rooted in the same cause: the model is forced to infer business semantics that the schema does not encode. We test whether supplying those semantics as context closes the gap. We benchmark three frontier LLMs (Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4) on 100 natural-language questions over the Cleaned Contoso Retail Dataset in ClickHouse, using a paired single-shot protocol. Each model is evaluated twice: once given only the warehouse schema, and once given the schema plus a 4 KB hand-authored markdown document describing the dataset's measures, conventions, and disambiguation rules. Adding the document improves accuracy by +17 to +23 percentage points across all three models. With it, the three models are statistically indistinguishable (67.7-68.7%); without it, they are also indistinguishable (45.5-50.5%). Every cross-cluster comparison is significant at p < 0.01. The presence of the semantic-layer document accounts for essentially all of the significant variance; model choice within tier does not. We interpret this as a structural result: explicit business semantics suppress the dominant class of text-to-SQL errors not by making the model more capable, but by changing what the model is being asked to do.

ai.tldr: 为LLM提供业务语义层文档可将分析查询准确率提升17-23个百分点，且模型间差异不显著。
ai.motivation: LLM在分析数据库的自然语言查询中常产生错误答案和自信幻觉，根源在于模型被迫推断模式未编码的业务语义。
ai.method: 在ClickHouse上的Contoso零售数据集上，用100个自然语言问题对三个前沿LLM进行配对单次测试，分别仅给模式或模式加4KB语义层文档。
ai.result: 添加文档后准确率提升17-23个百分点，三个模型性能无统计差异（67.7-68.7%）；无文档时也无差异（45.5-50.5%）。
ai.help_to_user: 说明在LLM数据分析中，提供明确的业务语义层比选择模型更重要，可指导实际系统设计。

idea_spark.idea: 探索自动生成或维护语义层文档的方法，减少人工编写成本。
idea_spark.risk: 语义层可能过时或不完整，需定期更新；依赖文档质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将业务知识显式注入LLM上下文，而非依赖模型隐式学习，可能是一种更可靠的范式。

en.tldr: Providing LLMs with a business semantic layer document improves analytical query accuracy by 17-23 percentage points, with no significant differences among models.
en.motivation: LLMs for natural-language querying of analytical databases produce incorrect answers and confident hallucinations because they must infer business semantics not encoded in the schema.
en.method: Paired single-shot benchmark on 100 questions over the Cleaned Contoso Retail Dataset in ClickHouse, testing three frontier LLMs with and without a 4 KB semantic-layer document.

idea_spark.idea: Investigate automatic generation or maintenance of semantic-layer documents to reduce manual effort.
idea_spark.risk: Semantic layers may become outdated or incomplete, requiring regular updates; reliance on document quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explicitly injecting business knowledge into LLM context, rather than relying on implicit learning, may offer a more reliable paradigm.

papers.title: spectroxide: A code package for computing cosmic microwave background spectral distortions
papers.summary: We present spectroxide, a code package for computing cosmic microwave background spectral distortions in which all ${\sim}14{,}500$ lines of Rust code, Python interface, and ${\sim}400$ automated tests were written by an AI assistant (Claude Code) under human physicist supervision. The solver evolves the photon Boltzmann equation under Compton scattering, double Compton emission, and Bremsstrahlung from $z \sim 5 \times 10^6$ to the present, computing spectral distortions from arbitrary heat and photon injection within this redshift range. No fully open-source code of this kind is publicly available; we validate against analytic limits, published spectra, and publicly available precomputed Green's function tables. We document the development as a case study in AI-assisted scientific computing, highlighting how domain expertise caught physics bugs (incorrect dimensional prefactors, near-cancellation errors) that evaded the full automated test suite, and provide recommendations for best practices in human--AI collaborative development of scientific software. We make spectroxide publicly available on GitHub.

ai.tldr: 本文介绍 spectroxide，一个由 AI 助手（Claude Code）在人类物理学家监督下编写的开源代码包，用于计算宇宙微波背景谱畸变。
ai.motivation: 目前缺乏完全开源的 CMB 谱畸变计算代码，现有工具不透明或不可用。
ai.method: 用 Rust 编写核心求解器，通过 Python 接口调用，求解光子玻尔兹曼方程，涵盖康普顿散射、双康普顿发射和轫致辐射过程。
ai.result: 代码通过了分析极限、已发表谱和预计算格林函数表的验证，并作为 AI 辅助科学计算的案例研究。
ai.help_to_user: 提供现成的开源工具，可计算任意热和光子注入下的 CMB 谱畸变，并展示 AI 辅助开发科学软件的最佳实践。

idea_spark.idea: 利用 AI 生成代码框架和测试，结合人类专家审查物理正确性，可加速科学软件开发。
idea_spark.risk: AI 可能引入难以发现的物理错误（如量纲错误），需要领域专家深度参与验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将 AI 代码生成与自动化测试、物理验证相结合，形成人机协作的软件开发流水线。

en.tldr: This paper presents spectroxide, an open-source code package for computing cosmic microwave background spectral distortions, written by an AI assistant (Claude Code) under human physicist supervision.
en.motivation: No fully open-source code of this kind is publicly available; existing tools are opaque or unavailable.
en.method: The solver is written in Rust with a Python interface, evolving the photon Boltzmann equation including Compton scattering, double Compton emission, and Bremsstrahlung.
en.result: The code is validated against analytic limits, published spectra, and precomputed Green's function tables, and serves as a case study in AI-assisted scientific computing.
en.help_to_user: Provides a ready-to-use open-source tool for computing CMB spectral distortions from arbitrary heat and photon injections, and demonstrates best practices for human-AI collaborative scientific software development.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage AI to generate code frameworks and tests, combined with human expert review of physical correctness, to accelerate scientific software development.
idea_spark.risk: AI may introduce subtle physics errors (e.g., dimensional prefactors) that evade automated tests, requiring deep domain expert involvement.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine AI code generation with automated testing and physics validation to form a human-AI collaborative software development pipeline.

papers.title: Vision SmolMamba: Spike-Guided Token Pruning for Energy-Efficient Spiking State-Space Vision Models
papers.summary: Spiking Transformers have shown strong potential for long-range visual modeling through spike-driven self-attention. However, their quadratic token interactions remain fundamentally misaligned with the sparse and event-driven nature of spiking neural computation. To address this limitation, we propose Vision SmolMamba, an energy-efficient spiking state-space architecture that integrates spike-driven dynamics with linear-time selective recurrence. The key idea is a Spike-Guided Spatio-Temporal Token Pruner (SST-TP), which estimates token importance using both spike activation strength and first-spike latency. This mechanism progressively removes redundant tokens while preserving salient spatio-temporal information, enabling efficient scaling with token sparsity. Based on this mechanism, the proposed SmolMamba block incorporates spike events directly into bidirectional state-space recurrence, forming a spiking state-space vision backbone for efficient long-range modeling. Extensive experiments on both static and event-based benchmarks, including ImageNet-1K, CIFAR10/100, CIFAR10-DVS, and DVS128 Gesture, demonstrate that Vision SmolMamba consistently achieves superior accuracy-efficiency trade-offs. In particular, it reduces the estimated energy cost by at least 1.5x compared with prior spiking Transformer baselines and a Spiking Mamba variant while maintaining competitive or improved accuracy. These results demonstrate that combining spike-guided token sparsity with state-space modeling offers a scalable and energy-efficient paradigm for spiking vision systems.

ai.tldr: 提出Vision SmolMamba，一种基于脉冲引导令牌修剪的节能脉冲状态空间视觉模型，在多个基准上实现优越的精度-效率权衡。
ai.motivation: 脉冲Transformer在长程视觉建模中表现优异，但其二次令牌交互与脉冲神经计算的稀疏事件驱动特性不匹配。
ai.method: 设计脉冲引导时空令牌修剪器（SST-TP），利用脉冲激活强度和首次脉冲延迟估计令牌重要性，逐步移除冗余令牌；并将脉冲事件直接集成到双向状态空间递归中形成SmolMamba块。
ai.result: 在ImageNet-1K、CIFAR10/100、CIFAR10-DVS和DVS128 Gesture上，相比先前脉冲Transformer和Spiking Mamba变体，能耗降低至少1.5倍，精度持平或提升。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种将脉冲引导令牌稀疏性与状态空间建模结合的节能视觉框架，适用于资源受限场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将脉冲引导修剪与动态计算图结合，实现自适应推理深度。
idea_spark.risk: 修剪策略可能丢失关键时空信息，需在稀疏性与准确性间仔细平衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受脉冲神经计算稀疏性启发，将状态空间模型与事件驱动机制融合，开辟高效视觉建模新路径。

zh.tldr: 提出Vision SmolMamba，一种基于脉冲引导令牌修剪的节能脉冲状态空间视觉模型，在多个基准上实现优越的精度-效率权衡。
zh.motivation: 脉冲Transformer在长程视觉建模中表现优异，但其二次令牌交互与脉冲神经计算的稀疏事件驱动特性不匹配。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将脉冲引导修剪与动态计算图结合，实现自适应推理深度。
idea_spark.risk: 修剪策略可能丢失关键时空信息，需在稀疏性与准确性间仔细平衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受脉冲神经计算稀疏性启发，将状态空间模型与事件驱动机制融合，开辟高效视觉建模新路径。

en.tldr: Proposes Vision SmolMamba, an energy-efficient spiking state-space model with spike-guided token pruning, achieving superior accuracy-efficiency trade-offs on multiple benchmarks.
en.motivation: Spiking Transformers excel in long-range visual modeling but their quadratic token interactions mismatch the sparse, event-driven nature of spiking neural computation.
en.method: Designs a Spike-Guided Spatio-Temporal Token Pruner (SST-TP) that estimates token importance via spike activation strength and first-spike latency, progressively removing redundant tokens; integrates spike events into bidirectional state-space recurrence to form the SmolMamba block.
en.result: On ImageNet-1K, CIFAR10/100, CIFAR10-DVS, and DVS128 Gesture, reduces estimated energy cost by at least 1.5x compared to prior spiking Transformers and a Spiking Mamba variant, while maintaining competitive or improved accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Provides an energy-efficient vision framework combining spike-guided token sparsity with state-space modeling, suitable for resource-constrained applications.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining spike-guided pruning with dynamic computation graphs for adaptive inference depth.
idea_spark.risk: Pruning strategy may lose critical spatio-temporal information, requiring careful balance between sparsity and accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the sparsity of spiking neural computation, fuses state-space models with event-driven mechanisms, opening a new path for efficient vision modeling.

papers.title: Decoding Delay Guarantees of Space Regulated Multiple Access Random Wireless Networks using Successive Interference Cancellation
papers.summary: This paper is focused on decoding delay guarantees in wireless networks, where messages have a given signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio threshold $η_0$ to meet in order to be successfully decoded, and where this should occur within some strict time constraints. Its main contribution consists in quantifying the worst-case transmissions decoding delays in the uplink of a cell-free network using successive interference cancellation. We show how such decoding delay guarantees can be obtained using spatial network calculus, a new tool introduced recently, and in particular spatial regulation.

ai.tldr: 本文量化了使用连续干扰消除的无蜂窝网络上行链路中最坏情况下的传输解码延迟，并展示了如何利用空间网络演算和空间调节来保证解码延迟。
ai.motivation: 无线网络中消息需要在严格时间约束内成功解码，但现有研究缺乏对最坏情况解码延迟的量化保证。
ai.method: 采用空间网络演算这一新工具，结合空间调节方法，分析随机无线网络中基于信号干扰噪声比阈值的解码延迟上界。
ai.result: 成功推导出在连续干扰消除下无蜂窝网络上行链路的最坏情况解码延迟保证。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助网络设计者理解并保证关键任务通信中的延迟上限。

idea_spark.idea: 将空间调节与连续干扰消除结合，为超可靠低延迟通信提供理论延迟保证。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设可能过于理想化，实际信道和干扰动态可能影响延迟保证的准确性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将空间网络演算用于物联网或车联网中的实时性分析。

zh.tldr: 本文量化了使用连续干扰消除的无蜂窝网络上行链路中最坏情况下的传输解码延迟，并展示了如何利用空间网络演算和空间调节来保证解码延迟。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助网络设计者理解并保证关键任务通信中的延迟上限。

idea_spark.idea: 将空间调节与连续干扰消除结合，为超可靠低延迟通信提供理论延迟保证。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设可能过于理想化，实际信道和干扰动态可能影响延迟保证的准确性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将空间网络演算用于物联网或车联网中的实时性分析。

en.tldr: This paper quantifies worst-case transmission decoding delays in the uplink of a cell-free network using successive interference cancellation, and shows how to guarantee such delays via spatial network calculus and spatial regulation.
en.motivation: Messages in wireless networks must be decoded within strict time constraints, but existing work lacks quantification of worst-case decoding delay guarantees.
en.method: Spatial network calculus, a recently introduced tool, combined with spatial regulation, is used to analyze decoding delay bounds based on signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio thresholds in random wireless networks.
en.result: Worst-case decoding delay guarantees are derived for cell-free network uplink with successive interference cancellation.
en.help_to_user: Helps network designers understand and guarantee delay bounds for mission-critical communications.

idea_spark.idea: Combining spatial regulation with successive interference cancellation provides theoretical delay guarantees for ultra-reliable low-latency communications.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions may be too idealistic; real-world channel and interference dynamics could affect the accuracy of delay guarantees.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the use of spatial network calculus for real-time analysis in IoT or vehicular networks.

papers.title: From Local Indices to Global Identifiers: Generative Reranking for Recommender Systems via Global Action Space
papers.summary: In modern recommender systems, list-wise reranking serves as a critical phase within the multi-stage pipeline, finalizing the exposed item sequence and directly impacting user satisfaction by modeling complex intra-list item dependencies. Existing methods typically formulate this task as selecting indices from the local input list. However, this approach suffers from a semantically inconsistent action space: the same output neuron (logits) represents different items across different samples, preventing the model from establishing a stable, intrinsic understanding of the items. To address this, we propose GloRank (Global Action Space Ranker), a generative framework that shifts reranking from selecting local indices to generating global identifiers. Specifically, we represent items as sequences of discrete tokens and reformulate reranking as a token generation task. This design effectively decouples the scoring mechanism from the variable input order, ensuring that items are evaluated against a consistent global standard. We further enhance this with a two-stage optimization pipeline: a supervised pre-training phase to initialize the model with high-quality demonstrations, followed by a reinforcement learning-based post-training phase to directly maximize list-wise utility. Extensive experiments on two public benchmarks and a large-scale industrial dataset, coupled with online A/B tests, demonstrate that GloRank consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines and achieves superior robustness in cold-start scenarios.

ai.tldr: 提出GloRank，将列表重排序从选择局部索引转变为生成全局标识符，通过两阶段优化（监督预训练+强化学习后训练）提升推荐系统性能。
ai.motivation: 现有列表重排序方法使用局部索引作为动作空间，导致语义不一致：同一输出神经元在不同样本中代表不同物品，模型无法建立稳定的物品理解。
ai.method: 将物品表示为离散token序列，重排序转化为token生成任务；采用两阶段流水线：监督预训练初始化模型，强化学习后训练直接最大化列表效用。
ai.result: 在公开基准、大规模工业数据集及在线A/B测试中，GloRank一致优于最先进基线，并在冷启动场景中表现出更强的鲁棒性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过生成式方法统一重排序的动作空间，提升推荐列表的整体质量和冷启动适应性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将物品的全局标识符与用户历史行为序列联合建模，实现端到端的生成式推荐。

en.tldr: Proposes GloRank, which shifts list-wise reranking from selecting local indices to generating global identifiers, using a two-stage pipeline (supervised pre-training + RL post-training) to improve recommender systems.
en.motivation: Existing list-wise reranking methods use local indices as action space, causing semantic inconsistency: the same output neuron represents different items across samples, preventing stable item understanding.
en.method: Represents items as sequences of discrete tokens, reformulating reranking as token generation; employs a two-stage pipeline: supervised pre-training to initialize the model, followed by RL post-training to directly maximize list-wise utility.
en.result: GloRank consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on public benchmarks, large-scale industrial datasets, and online A/B tests, with superior robustness in cold-start scenarios.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how a generative approach unifies the action space for reranking, improving overall list quality and cold-start adaptability.

idea_spark.idea: Explore joint modeling of global item identifiers with user historical behavior sequences for end-to-end generative recommendation.
idea_spark.risk: Discrete token representation may lose fine-grained item semantics, and generative decoding incurs high computational cost.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by sequence generation and RL alignment in NLP, treating recommendation reranking as a structured output generation problem.

papers.title: DouC: Dual-Branch CLIP for Training-Free Open-Vocabulary Segmentation
papers.summary: Open-vocabulary semantic segmentation requires assigning pixel-level semantic labels while supporting an open and unrestricted set of categories. Training-free CLIP-based approaches preserve strong zero-shot generalization but typically rely on a single inference mechanism, limiting their ability to jointly address unreliable local tokens and insufficient spatial coherence. We propose DouC, a training-free dual-branch CLIP framework that decomposes dense prediction into two complementary components. OG-CLIP improves patch-level reliability via lightweight, inference-time token gating, while FADE-CLIP injects external structural priors through proxy attention guided by frozen vision foundation models. The two branches are fused at the logit level, enabling local token reliability and structure-aware patch interactions to jointly influence final predictions, with optional instance-aware correction applied as post-processing. DouC introduces no additional learnable parameters, requires no retraining, and preserves CLIP's zero-shot generalization. Extensive experiments across eight benchmarks and multiple CLIP backbones demonstrate that DouC consistently outperforms prior training-free methods and scales favorably with model capacity.

ai.tldr: 提出DouC，一种无需训练的双分支CLIP框架，通过互补分支融合实现开放词汇语义分割，保持零样本泛化能力。
ai.motivation: 现有无训练CLIP方法依赖单一推理机制，无法同时解决局部token不可靠和空间连贯性不足的问题。
ai.method: 构建双分支结构：OG-CLIP通过推理时token门控提升局部可靠性，FADE-CLIP利用冻结视觉基础模型的代理注意力注入结构先验，最后在logit层融合两分支输出，并可选实例感知后处理。
ai.result: 在八个基准和多种CLIP骨干上，DouC一致优于先前无训练方法，且随模型容量提升性能更好。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需额外训练即可提升开放词汇分割质量的方法，适合快速部署和零样本场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将OG-CLIP的token门控与FADE-CLIP的代理注意力结合到统一注意力机制中，而非简单logit融合。
idea_spark.risk: 双分支可能增加推理计算开销，且对视觉基础模型的选择敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用冻结基础模型的结构先验来引导CLIP的语义理解，启发其他多模态对齐任务。

en.tldr: Proposes DouC, a training-free dual-branch CLIP framework that fuses complementary branches for open-vocabulary segmentation while preserving zero-shot generalization.
en.motivation: Existing training-free CLIP methods rely on a single inference mechanism, failing to jointly address unreliable local tokens and insufficient spatial coherence.
en.method: Builds a dual-branch architecture: OG-CLIP improves patch-level reliability via inference-time token gating, and FADE-CLIP injects structural priors through proxy attention guided by frozen vision foundation models; the two branches are fused at the logit level, with optional instance-aware post-processing.
en.result: On eight benchmarks and multiple CLIP backbones, DouC consistently outperforms prior training-free methods and scales favorably with model capacity.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method to improve open-vocabulary segmentation quality without additional training, suitable for rapid deployment and zero-shot scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Explore integrating OG-CLIP's token gating and FADE-CLIP's proxy attention into a unified attention mechanism rather than simple logit fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Dual branches may increase inference computational cost and are sensitive to the choice of vision foundation models.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using structural priors from frozen foundation models to guide CLIP's semantic understanding inspires other multimodal alignment tasks.

papers.title: Computing welfare and fairness in allocating identical goods with entitlements and general utility functions
papers.summary: A number of goods are called identical if they provide the same level of utility to each agent. In various real-world instances of fair division scenarios, identical indivisible items are allocated to consumers or demandants with different entitlements. We assume that the utility of $t$ identical items to each agent $A$ equals $f_A(t)$, where $f_A$ is an arbitrary increasing function associated with $A$. We present a polynomial time algorithm that determines the maximum weighted Rawlsian and Leximin welfare for scenarios with identical goods and show that the allocation obtained by the algorithm is equitable up to any item (WEQX). Some results concerning restricted utilitarian welfare and the existence of WEFX allocations are also presented. We introduce a new quantity ``total weighted deficit," for allocations, and by which we obtain a tractable algorithm to achieve equitable allocations for scenarios with identical goods and different entitlements via compensation by using a minimum number of identical coins.

ai.tldr: 针对具有不同权利和一般效用函数的同质物品分配问题，提出多项式时间算法计算最大加权Rawlsian和Leximin福利，并实现公平分配。
ai.motivation: 现实中的公平分配场景常涉及同质不可分物品和不同权利，现有方法难以处理一般效用函数下的福利最大化与公平性。
ai.method: 设计多项式时间算法，引入“总加权赤字”概念，通过最小数量同质硬币补偿实现公平分配。
ai.result: 算法能确定最大加权Rawlsian和Leximin福利，分配满足WEQX性质，并给出受限功利福利和WEFX存在性的结果。
ai.help_to_user: 为具有不同权利和一般效用函数的同质物品分配提供可计算的最优公平方案。

idea_spark.idea: 利用最小补偿机制实现公平，可启发设计基于补偿的公平分配协议。
idea_spark.risk: 补偿机制可能增加系统复杂度或资源消耗，需权衡效率与公平。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将赤字最小化与福利最大化结合，为多目标优化提供新视角。

en.tldr: A polynomial-time algorithm computes maximum weighted Rawlsian and Leximin welfare for allocating identical indivisible goods to agents with different entitlements and general utility functions, achieving equitable allocations via compensation.
en.motivation: Real-world fair division often involves identical indivisible goods and agents with different entitlements, but existing methods struggle with general utility functions and welfare maximization.
en.method: A polynomial-time algorithm is designed, introducing the concept of 'total weighted deficit' and using a minimum number of identical coins for compensation to achieve equitable allocations.
en.result: The algorithm determines maximum weighted Rawlsian and Leximin welfare, yields WEQX allocations, and provides results on restricted utilitarian welfare and existence of WEFX allocations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a computationally tractable optimal fair allocation scheme for identical goods with entitlements and general utilities.

idea_spark.idea: Using minimal compensation to achieve fairness can inspire the design of compensation-based fair allocation protocols.
idea_spark.risk: Compensation mechanisms may increase system complexity or resource consumption, requiring a trade-off between efficiency and fairness.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining deficit minimization with welfare maximization offers a new perspective for multi-objective optimization.

papers.title: Liouville Blocks from Spectral Networks
papers.summary: In this paper, we investigate the role of spectral networks in quantum Liouville theory, with particular emphasis on spectral networks of Fenchel-Nielsen-type. In the first part, we construct q-parallel transport for Fenchel-Nielsen networks through q-nonabelianisation, and compare with quantum parallel transport computed using the Moore-Seiberg formalism. This motivates a proposal for a quantum version of the NRS proposal. In the second part, we reproduce Liouville conformal blocks through the standard free-field formalism with Fenchel-Nielsen-type integration contours. However, we observe that this approach is not complete with respect to wall-crossing. We therefore develop an extension of the free-field formalism to smooth spectral coverings, with the Maulik-Okounkov R-matrix playing a central role. We conjecture that this new formalism generates the full spectrum of Liouville conformal blocks, and provides a first-principle definition for Goncharov-Shen conformal blocks.

ai.tldr: 本文研究谱网络在量子Liouville理论中的作用，构造了Fenchel-Nielsen型网络的q-平行输运，并发展了光滑谱覆盖的自由场形式，以完整生成Liouville共形块。
ai.motivation: 现有自由场形式在墙交叉下不完整，需要扩展以覆盖全部Liouville共形块谱。
ai.method: 通过q-非阿贝尔化构造q-平行输运，与Moore-Seiberg形式比较；引入Maulik-Okounkov R-矩阵扩展自由场形式到光滑谱覆盖。
ai.result: 提出了量子NRS提议的量子版本，并猜想新形式能生成完整Liouville共形块谱，为Goncharov-Shen共形块提供第一性原理定义。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解谱网络与量子Liouville理论的关系，以及共形块计算的完整框架。

idea_spark.idea: 利用光滑谱覆盖和R-矩阵构造完整共形块基，可能解决墙交叉问题。
idea_spark.risk: 猜想需要严格证明，且计算复杂度可能较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将几何结构（谱网络）与代数结构（R-矩阵）结合，为共形块提供新视角。

en.tldr: This paper investigates spectral networks in quantum Liouville theory, constructs q-parallel transport for Fenchel-Nielsen networks, and develops a free-field formalism on smooth spectral coverings to generate the full spectrum of Liouville conformal blocks.
en.motivation: The standard free-field formalism is incomplete under wall-crossing, requiring an extension to cover all Liouville conformal blocks.
en.method: Construct q-parallel transport via q-nonabelianisation and compare with Moore-Seiberg formalism; extend free-field formalism to smooth spectral coverings using the Maulik-Okounkov R-matrix.
en.result: Proposes a quantum version of the NRS proposal and conjectures that the new formalism generates the full spectrum of Liouville conformal blocks, providing a first-principle definition for Goncharov-Shen conformal blocks.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the role of spectral networks in quantum Liouville theory and provides a complete framework for computing conformal blocks.

idea_spark.idea: Use smooth spectral coverings and R-matrices to construct a complete basis of conformal blocks, potentially resolving wall-crossing issues.
idea_spark.risk: The conjecture requires rigorous proof, and computational complexity may be high.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining geometric structures (spectral networks) with algebraic structures (R-matrices) offers a new perspective on conformal blocks.

papers.title: Semi-Markov Reinforcement Learning for City-Scale EV Ride-Hailing with Feasibility-Guaranteed Actions
papers.summary: We study city-scale control of electric-vehicle (EV) ride-hailing fleets where dispatch, repositioning, and charging decisions must respect charger and feeder limits under uncertain, spatially correlated demand and travel times. We formulate the problem as a hex-grid semi-Markov decision process (semi-MDP) with mixed actions -- discrete actions for serving, repositioning, and charging, together with continuous charging power -- and variable action durations. To guarantee physical feasibility during both training and deployment, the policy learns over high-level intentions produced by a masked, temperature-annealed actor. These intentions are projected at every decision step through a time-limited rolling mixed-integer linear program (MILP) that strictly enforces state-of-charge, port, and feeder constraints. To mitigate distributional shifts, we optimize a Soft Actor--Critic (SAC) agent against a Wasserstein-1 ambiguity set with a graph-aligned Mahalanobis ground metric that captures spatial correlations. The robust backup uses the Kantorovich--Rubinstein dual, a projected subgradient inner loop, and a primal--dual risk-budget update. Our architecture combines a two-layer Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) encoder, twin critics, and a value network that drives the adversary. Experiments on a large-scale EV fleet simulator built from NYC taxi data show that PD--RSAC achieves the highest net profit, reaching \$1.22M, compared with \$0.58M--\$0.70M for strong heuristic, single-agent RL, and multi-agent RL baselines, including Greedy, SAC, MAPPO, and MADDPG, while maintaining zero feeder-limit violations.

ai.tldr: 提出一种半马尔可夫强化学习方法，用于城市级电动汽车网约车车队控制，通过混合整数线性规划保证动作可行性，并利用鲁棒软演员-评论家算法应对分布偏移。
ai.motivation: 电动汽车网约车车队在城市规模下需同时考虑派单、重新定位和充电决策，且受限于充电桩和馈线容量，现有方法难以保证物理可行性并应对不确定需求与旅行时间。
ai.method: 将问题建模为六边形网格半马尔可夫决策过程，策略输出高层意图后通过带时间限制的混合整数线性规划投影为可行动作；采用基于Wasserstein-1模糊集和Mahalanobis距离的鲁棒软演员-评论家算法优化，结合图卷积网络编码器和原始-对偶风险预算更新。
ai.result: 在基于纽约出租车数据的大规模仿真中，所提方法实现最高净利润122万美元，远超基线方法（58万-70万美元），且零馈线违规。
ai.help_to_user: 为电动汽车网约车平台提供一种可保证物理约束的强化学习调度方案，提升运营效率与鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 将动作投影与鲁棒优化结合，利用图结构捕捉空间相关性，为高维约束下的强化学习提供新范式。
idea_spark.risk: 混合整数线性规划求解可能带来计算延迟，需优化求解效率以适配实时决策。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受鲁棒优化和半马尔可夫过程启发，将可行性保证与分布鲁棒性统一在强化学习框架中。

en.tldr: Proposes a semi-Markov reinforcement learning method for city-scale EV ride-hailing fleet control, ensuring action feasibility via mixed-integer linear programming and addressing distributional shifts with robust soft actor-critic.
en.motivation: City-scale EV ride-hailing requires joint dispatch, repositioning, and charging decisions under charger/feeder limits and uncertain demand/travel times; existing methods struggle with physical feasibility and robustness.
en.method: Formulates a hex-grid semi-MDP with mixed actions; policy outputs high-level intentions projected to feasible actions via a time-limited MILP. Optimizes a robust SAC agent with a Wasserstein-1 ambiguity set and graph-aligned Mahalanobis metric, using GCN encoder and primal-dual risk budget update.
en.result: In large-scale simulations based on NYC taxi data, achieves highest net profit of $1.22M vs. $0.58M–$0.70M for baselines, with zero feeder violations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a reinforcement learning solution for EV ride-hailing platforms that guarantees physical constraint satisfaction, improving operational efficiency and robustness.

idea_spark.idea: Combines action projection with robust optimization, leveraging graph structure for spatial correlations, offering a new paradigm for constrained RL.
idea_spark.risk: MILP solving may introduce computational latency; optimization for real-time decision-making is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by robust optimization and semi-Markov processes, unifying feasibility guarantees and distributional robustness in RL.

papers.title: Central Limit Theorem for Mutation Systems
papers.summary: DNA-based storage has emerged as a promising alternative to traditional data storage methods, offering unmatched advantages in data density, longevity, and sustainability. Two main approaches have developed: in-vitro storage, where information is synthesized in controlled environments, and in-vivo storage, where data is embedded within an organism's DNA for enhanced confidentiality and protection. While in-vivo DNA storage provides unique advantages, it faces significant challenges from mutations, including duplications, deletions, and substitutions, which cause sequence evolution over time. Thus, in-vivo systems experience continuous sequence alterations that increase length and change composition, making error correction particularly challenging.   We study the asymptotic behavior of mutation systems, which model the probabilistic evolution of sequences over a finite alphabet, and are central to the analysis of in-vivo DNA-based data storage. Building upon prior works that established the limit of empirical $k$-tuple frequencies, we characterize the stochastic fluctuations around these values by establishing a Central Limit Theorem (CLT). Our approach leverages the spectral properties of the $k$-substitution matrix to project the centered count vectors, allowing us to approximate the system via a martingale difference sequence, and then verifying the classical martingale CLT conditions. In addition, we explicitly derive the limiting covariance matrix.

ai.tldr: 本文针对体内DNA存储中的突变系统，建立了中心极限定理，刻画了序列k元组频率的随机波动。
ai.motivation: 体内DNA存储面临突变挑战，导致序列不断变化，错误校正困难，需要理解突变系统的渐近行为。
ai.method: 利用k-替换矩阵的谱性质投影中心化计数向量，通过鞅差序列逼近系统，并验证经典鞅中心极限定理条件。
ai.result: 证明了突变系统的中心极限定理，并显式推导了极限协方差矩阵。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解体内DNA存储中序列演化的统计规律，为错误校正和存储可靠性分析提供理论基础。

idea_spark.idea: 将中心极限定理应用于设计基于突变统计特征的纠错码，或用于预测长期存储中数据丢失概率。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设可能过于简化真实突变过程（如非均匀突变率），实际应用需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于将随机过程理论用于生物信息存储，可进一步探索突变系统的遍历性和大偏差原理。

en.motivation: In-vivo DNA storage faces mutation challenges causing continuous sequence changes, making error correction difficult; understanding asymptotic behavior of mutation systems is essential.

idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by applying stochastic process theory to bioinformatics storage; further exploration of ergodicity and large deviation principles for mutation systems is possible.

papers.title: Combating Visual Neglect and Semantic Drift in Large Multimodal Models for Enhanced Cross-Modal Retrieval
papers.summary: Despite significant progress in Unified Multimodal Retrieval (UMR) powered by Large Multimodal Models (LMMs), existing embedding methods primarily focus on sample-level objectives via contrastive learning while overlooking the crucial subject-level semantics. This limitation hinders the model's ability to group semantically coherent subjects in complex multimodal queries, manifesting as semantic alignment deviation--where models fail to accurately localize salient text-referred regions in visual content. Moreover, without explicit guidance to model salient visual subjects, LMMs tend to over-rely on textual cues, resulting in visual modality neglect and suboptimal utilization of visual knowledge. To this end, we propose Salient Subject-Aware Multimodal Embedding (SSA-ME), a novel framework designed to enhance fine-grained representation learning through saliency-aware modeling. SSA-ME leverages LMMs and visual experts to identify and emphasize salient visual concepts in image-text pairs, and introduces a saliency-guided objective to better align cross-modal attention with semantically meaningful regions. Additionally, a feature regeneration module recalibrates visual features based on the derived saliency maps, ensuring a balanced and semantically coherent integration across modalities. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MMEB benchmark, demonstrating that incorporating subject-level modeling substantially improves multimodal retrieval. Comprehensive qualitative analyses further illustrate the interpretability and effectiveness of our approach.

ai.tldr: 提出SSA-ME框架，通过显著性感知建模增强大型多模态模型的细粒度表示学习，解决视觉忽视和语义漂移问题，在MMEB基准上取得最优性能。
ai.motivation: 现有嵌入方法仅关注样本级对比学习，忽略主体级语义，导致模型在复杂多模态查询中无法准确对齐文本所指的视觉区域，且过度依赖文本线索而忽视视觉模态。
ai.method: 利用LMM和视觉专家识别并强调图像-文本对中的显著视觉概念，引入显著性引导目标对齐跨模态注意力，并通过特征再生模块基于显著性图重新校准视觉特征。
ai.result: 在MMEB基准上达到最先进性能，定性分析验证了方法的可解释性和有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过主体级建模提升多模态检索的准确性和鲁棒性，尤其适用于需要细粒度语义对齐的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将显著性图与特征再生结合，动态调整视觉特征权重，可能启发更通用的模态平衡机制。
idea_spark.risk: 显著性检测的准确性依赖预训练模型，可能引入噪声；特征再生模块增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类视觉注意力机制出发，模拟对显著区域的优先处理，可推广至多模态理解的其他子领域。

idea_spark.risk: Saliency detection accuracy depends on pre-trained models and may introduce noise; feature regeneration module adds computational overhead.

papers.title: PSI-Bench: Towards Clinically Grounded and Interpretable Evaluation of Depression Patient Simulators
papers.summary: Patient simulators are gaining traction in mental health training by providing scalable exposure to complex and sensitive patient interactions. Simulating depressed patients is particularly challenging, as safety constraints and high patient variability complicate simulations and underscore the need for simulators that capture diverse and realistic patient behaviors. However, existing evaluations heavily rely on LLM-judges with poorly specified prompts and do not assess behavioral diversity. We introduce PSI-Bench, an automatic evaluation framework that provides interpretable, clinically grounded diagnostics of depression patient simulator behavior across turn-, dialogue-, and population-level dimensions. Using PSI-Bench, we benchmark seven LLMs across two simulator frameworks and find that simulators produce overly long, lexically diverse responses, show reduced variability, resolve emotions too quickly, and follow a uniform negative-to-positive trajectory. We also show that the simulation framework has a larger impact on fidelity than the model scale. Results from a human study demonstrate that our benchmark is strongly aligned with expert judgments. Our work reveals key limitations of current depression patient simulators and provides an interpretable, extensible benchmark to guide future simulator design and evaluation.

ai.tldr: 提出PSI-Bench框架，用于临床可解释地评估抑郁症患者模拟器，发现现有模拟器存在响应过长、多样性不足、情绪恢复过快等问题。
ai.motivation: 现有抑郁症患者模拟器评估依赖LLM评判且缺乏行为多样性评估，需要更临床可解释的自动评估方法。
ai.method: 构建PSI-Bench基准，从回合、对话、群体三个维度提供可解释诊断，并基于7个LLM和两个模拟框架进行基准测试。
ai.result: 模拟器产生过长且词汇多样的响应，变异性降低，情绪过快从负面转为正面；模拟框架对保真度影响大于模型规模；人类研究显示基准与专家判断高度一致。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员识别当前抑郁症患者模拟器的关键局限，并提供可扩展的评估基准以指导未来设计。

idea_spark.idea: 结合情绪轨迹分析和对话长度控制，设计更真实且安全的抑郁症患者模拟器。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟器可能过度简化患者行为，导致训练效果偏差；临床验证需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从临床心理学中患者行为变异性研究出发，引入动态情绪调节模型到模拟器中。

papers.title: Emergent Features in $U(N) \times U(\tilde{N})$ Bi-adjoint Cubic Theory
papers.summary: This work investigates the role of the $U(N) \times U(\tilde{N})$ global symmetry in tree-level scattering amplitudes of the bi-adjoint $φ^3$ theory from three perspectives: combinatorics, correlation functions, and a massive extension of the CHY formalism. We derive a planar scattering potential whose extrema reproduce Dolan and Goddard's massive scattering equations, providing physical intuition of the construction. This potential enables the counting of kinematic invariants via maximally symmetric Ferrers shapes, and it is expressed in terms of conformally invariant cross-ratios. We find that the $U(1)$ decoupling identity provides a physical interpretation of two different Catalan recursion relations, and also reveals an interplay between Catalan and Narayana numbers in the $U(1)$ splitting. Finally, we construct correlation functions for a fixed particle ordering using the CHY formalism, offering new insights into the dynamics of such amplitude structures. We derive a closed form expression of the reduced number of solutions for this set-up, as well as an off-shell scattering potential.

ai.tldr: 研究了双伴随φ³理论中U(N)×U(Ñ)全局对称性在树级散射振幅中的三个视角：组合学、关联函数和CHY形式的大质量扩展。
ai.motivation: 揭示U(N)×U(Ñ)对称性如何影响散射振幅的结构，并建立物理直觉。
ai.method: 推导了平面散射势，其极值重现了Dolan和Goddard的大质量散射方程；利用Ferrers形状计数运动学不变量；通过CHY形式构造固定粒子顺序的关联函数。
ai.result: U(1)解耦恒等式给出了两种Catalan递归关系的物理解释，并揭示了Catalan数与Narayana数在U(1)分裂中的相互作用；得到了该设置下解数目的闭式表达式以及离壳散射势。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解双伴随φ³理论中对称性对振幅组合结构和CHY形式的影响。

idea_spark.idea: 将U(1)解耦恒等式与Catalan递归关系的联系应用于更一般的振幅递归构造。
idea_spark.risk: 大质量扩展可能仅在树级有效，圈级推广需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从组合数（Catalan、Narayana）与振幅结构的关联中，可探索其他组合恒等式的物理对应。

papers.title: The mixed-dimensional quantum MacWilliams identity: bounds for codes and absolutely maximally entangled states in heterogeneous systems
papers.summary: As emerging quantum architectures evolve into heterogeneous networks combining different physical substrates, such as qubits for logic and higher-dimensional qudits for robust communication, the traditional scalar metrics of quantum error correction become insufficient. To address this, we introduce a mathematical framework based on dimension multisets to characterize quantum error-correcting codes (QECC) and absolutely maximally entangled (AME) states in mixed-dimensional Hilbert spaces. By replacing scalar weights with multisets, we accurately capture the exact physical composition of error supports across these diverse systems. Our central result is the mixed-dimensional quantum MacWilliams identity, which establishes the formal algebraic relationship between Shor-Laflamme enumerators and unitary weight enumerators. From this foundation, we deduce the mixed-dimensional shadow identity and derive rigorous, generalized constraints on code parameters, explicitly formulating the mixed-dimensional quantum Hamming, Singleton and Scott bounds, and developing a linear program to systematically evaluate code viability. For the Singleton bound, a tighter bound that has no homogeneous analogue is derived for pure mixed-dimensional codes. Finally, we deploy this enumerator machinery to thoroughly analyze AME states, utilizing shadow inequalities to constrain their existence and introducing a combinatorial grid method for the explicit construction of mixed-dimensional tripartite AME states.

ai.tldr: 提出混合维度量子MacWilliams恒等式，为异构量子系统中的量子纠错码和绝对最大纠缠态建立数学框架。
ai.motivation: 传统标量度量无法刻画混合维度希尔伯特空间中量子纠错码的精确物理组成。
ai.method: 引入维度多重集替代标量权重，推导混合维度量子MacWilliams恒等式，并由此得到影子恒等式及广义约束。
ai.result: 推导出混合维度量子Hamming、Singleton和Scott界，开发线性规划评估码可行性，并给出纯混合维度码的更紧Singleton界。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将经典编码理论中的MacWilliams恒等式推广到混合维度量子场景。

en.tldr: Proposes the mixed-dimensional quantum MacWilliams identity, establishing a mathematical framework for quantum error-correcting codes and absolutely maximally entangled states in heterogeneous quantum systems.
en.motivation: Traditional scalar metrics fail to capture the exact physical composition of error supports in mixed-dimensional Hilbert spaces.
en.method: Introduces dimension multisets to replace scalar weights, derives the mixed-dimensional quantum MacWilliams identity, and from it obtains the shadow identity and generalized constraints.
en.result: Derives mixed-dimensional quantum Hamming, Singleton, and Scott bounds, develops a linear program for code viability, and obtains a tighter Singleton bound for pure mixed-dimensional codes.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand error correction capabilities and existence constraints of entangled states in mixed-dimensional quantum systems.

idea_spark.idea: Uses a combinatorial grid method to explicitly construct mixed-dimensional tripartite absolutely maximally entangled states.
idea_spark.risk: Construction complexity may increase sharply with growing dimensions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extends the classical MacWilliams identity from coding theory to mixed-dimensional quantum scenarios.

papers.title: Praxy Voice: Voice-Prompt Recovery + BUPS for Commercial-Class Indic TTS from a Frozen Non-Indic Base at Zero Commercial-Training-Data Cost
papers.summary: Commercial TTS systems produce near-native Indic audio, but the best open-source bases (Chatterbox, Indic Parler-TTS, IndicF5) trail them on measured phonological dimensions, and the most widely adopted multilingual base (Chatterbox, 23 languages) does not even tokenise Telugu or Tamil. We ask: what is the minimum intervention that brings such a non-Indic-native base to commercial-class output on Telugu, Tamil, and Hindi, without training a new acoustic decoder and without any commercial TTS training data? We combine three pieces: (1) BUPS, a Brahmic Unified Phoneme Space that deterministically romanises seven Indic scripts to ISO-15919 so Chatterbox's Latin tokeniser can process them; (2) a LoRA adapter on only the text-token predictor (Chatterbox's t3), trained on ~1,220h of licensed Indic audio with a Hindi-proxy language_id; (3) a voice-prompt recovery recipe -- an 8-11s same-language reference clip plus three sampling overrides (exaggeration 0.7, temperature 0.6, min_p 0.1; "Config B") -- that recovers commercial-class acoustic output with no acoustic-decoder training. On Hindi, the LoRA regresses accuracy and we instead use vanilla Chatterbox + Config B, giving a two-branch deployment. Evaluated on 10-utterance pilot sets with the companion PSP benchmark, Praxy Voice matches or slightly leads commercial baselines: 26.7% retroflex collapse on Telugu (vs Sarvam Bulbul 33.3%), 71% Tamil-zha collapse (vs commercial trio's 86%), 0.025 LLM-WER on Hindi (tied with Cartesia Sonic-3). For intra-sentential code-mix we add a third branch (IndicF5 + native-script transliteration) that drops code-mix LLM-WER from 0.80-0.85 to 0.14-0.27 across Hi/Te/Ta. We release R6 LoRA weights (Apache-2.0), inference code and router (MIT), and a Gradio demo.

ai.tldr: 提出一种无需商业训练数据、仅通过最小干预（BUPS音素映射+LoRA适配+语音提示恢复）使非印度语基座模型达到商业级印度语TTS性能的方法。
ai.motivation: 现有开源印度语TTS模型在音位准确性上落后于商业系统，且主流多语言基座Chatterbox不支持泰卢固语和泰米尔语。
ai.method: 结合BUPS统一音素空间、仅训练文本令牌预测器的LoRA适配器、以及语音提示恢复配方（8-11秒参考音频+特定采样参数）。
ai.result: 在泰卢固语、泰米尔语和印地语上匹配或超越商业基线，如泰卢固语卷舌音崩溃率26.7%（商业33.3%），印地语LLM-WER 0.025（持平商业）。
ai.help_to_user: 为低资源印度语TTS提供零商业数据成本的实用方案，可直接部署或作为研究基线。

idea_spark.idea: 能否将语音提示恢复配方与更轻量的适配器（如AdapterFusion）结合，实现多语言零样本TTS？
idea_spark.risk: 依赖特定基座模型（Chatterbox）的文本令牌预测器，可能限制泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过音素层标准化绕过基座模型的语言限制，类似方法可用于跨语言语音识别或翻译。

zh.result: 在泰卢固语、泰米尔语和印地语上匹配或超越商业基线，如泰卢固语卷舌音崩溃率26.7%（商业33.3%），印地语LLM-WER 0.025（持平商业）。
zh.help_to_user: 为低资源印度语TTS提供零商业数据成本的实用方案，可直接部署或作为研究基线。

idea_spark.idea: 能否将语音提示恢复配方与更轻量的适配器（如AdapterFusion）结合，实现多语言零样本TTS？
idea_spark.risk: 依赖特定基座模型（Chatterbox）的文本令牌预测器，可能限制泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过音素层标准化绕过基座模型的语言限制，类似方法可用于跨语言语音识别或翻译。

en.tldr: A minimal-intervention approach (BUPS phoneme mapping + LoRA adapter + voice-prompt recovery) brings a non-Indic TTS base to commercial-class Indic TTS without any commercial training data.
en.motivation: Open-source Indic TTS lags behind commercial systems in phonological accuracy, and the popular multilingual base Chatterbox lacks tokenization for Telugu and Tamil.
en.method: Combines BUPS (Brahmic Unified Phoneme Space) for deterministic romanization, a LoRA adapter on the text-token predictor trained on ~1,220h of licensed Indic audio, and a voice-prompt recovery recipe (8-11s reference clip with specific sampling parameters).
en.result: Matches or exceeds commercial baselines on Telugu, Tamil, and Hindi: e.g., 26.7% retroflex collapse on Telugu (vs 33.3% commercial), 0.025 LLM-WER on Hindi (tied with Cartesia Sonic-3).
en.help_to_user: Provides a zero-cost, deployable solution for high-quality Indic TTS using only open-source components, useful for low-resource language applications.

idea_spark.idea: Can the voice-prompt recovery be combined with lighter adapters (e.g., AdapterFusion) for multilingual zero-shot TTS?
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on Chatterbox's text-token predictor may limit generalization to other base models.
idea_spark.inspiration: Standardizing at the phoneme level to bypass base model language constraints could be applied to cross-lingual ASR or translation.

papers.title: Training Transformers as a Universal Computer
papers.summary: We demonstrate that a small transformer can learn to execute programs in MicroPy, a simplified yet computationally universal programming language. Given procedure definitions together with an expression to evaluate, the transformer predicts small-step execution using PENCIL scaffolding for space-efficient execution within a bounded context window. After training on randomly generated, meaningless MicroPy programs, the learned transformer generalizes to various human-written programs including bit copying and flipping, binary addition and multiplication, and SAT verification and solving. We note that the trained model can achieve out-of-distribution generalization; i.e., evaluate novel programs from distribution on programs. Since MicroPy can express any computation, our results provide empirical evidence that a standard transformer can be trained to act as a universal computer.

ai.tldr: 本文证明一个小型Transformer可以学习执行MicroPy语言中的程序，并在未见过的程序上实现泛化，从而充当通用计算机。
ai.motivation: 探索标准Transformer能否被训练成通用计算机，即能够执行任意计算。
ai.method: 使用PENCIL脚手架在有限上下文窗口内进行空间高效的小步执行，并在随机生成的MicroPy程序上训练Transformer。
ai.result: 训练后的Transformer能够泛化到各种人工编写的程序，包括位复制、翻转、二进制加法、乘法以及SAT验证与求解，并展现出分布外泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解Transformer在程序执行和通用计算方面的潜力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Transformer执行程序的能力，可应用于代码理解、自动编程或形式验证。
idea_spark.risk: 模型可能对训练分布外的程序泛化能力有限，且执行效率受上下文窗口限制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将Transformer视为通用计算引擎，结合程序合成或神经符号方法。

zh.tldr: 本文证明一个小型Transformer可以学习执行MicroPy语言中的程序，并在未见过的程序上实现泛化，从而充当通用计算机。
zh.motivation: 探索标准Transformer能否被训练成通用计算机，即能够执行任意计算。
zh.method: 使用PENCIL脚手架在有限上下文窗口内进行空间高效的小步执行，并在随机生成的MicroPy程序上训练Transformer。
zh.result: 训练后的Transformer能够泛化到各种人工编写的程序，包括位复制、翻转、二进制加法、乘法以及SAT验证与求解，并展现出分布外泛化能力。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解Transformer在程序执行和通用计算方面的潜力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Transformer执行程序的能力，可应用于代码理解、自动编程或形式验证。
idea_spark.risk: 模型可能对训练分布外的程序泛化能力有限，且执行效率受上下文窗口限制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将Transformer视为通用计算引擎，结合程序合成或神经符号方法。

en.tldr: This paper shows that a small transformer can learn to execute MicroPy programs and generalize to unseen programs, effectively acting as a universal computer.
en.motivation: To investigate whether a standard transformer can be trained to serve as a universal computer capable of executing arbitrary computations.
en.method: Using PENCIL scaffolding for space-efficient small-step execution within a bounded context window, training on randomly generated MicroPy programs.
en.result: The trained transformer generalizes to various human-written programs including bit copying, flipping, binary addition, multiplication, and SAT verification/solving, demonstrating out-of-distribution generalization.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the potential of transformers for program execution and universal computation.

idea_spark.idea: Leveraging transformer program execution for code understanding, automated programming, or formal verification.
idea_spark.risk: Limited generalization to programs far from training distribution and efficiency constraints due to context window.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires viewing transformers as universal computing engines, combined with program synthesis or neuro-symbolic methods.

papers.title: AHASD: Asynchronous Heterogeneous Architecture for LLM Adaptive Drafting Speculative Decoding on Mobile Devices
papers.summary: Speculative decoding enhances the inference efficiency of large language models (LLMs) by generating drafts using a small draft language model (DLM) and verifying them in batches with a large target language model (TLM). However, adaptive drafting inference on a mobile single-NPU-PIM system faces idle overhead in traditional operator-level synchronous execution and wasted computation in asynchronous execution due to fluctuations in draft length. This paper introduces AHASD, a task-level asynchronous mobile NPU-PIM heterogeneous architecture for speculative decoding. Notably, AHASD achieves parallel drafting on the PIM and verification on a single NPU through task-level DLM-TLM decoupling and specifically, it incorporates Entropy-History-Aware Drafting Control and Time-Aware Pre-Verification Control to dynamically manage adaptive drafting algorithm execution and pre-verification timing, suppressing invalid drafting based on low-confidence drafts. Additionally, AHASD integrates Attention Algorithm Units and Gated Task Scheduling Units within LPDDR5-PIM to enable attention link localization and sub-microsecond task switching on the PIM side. Experimental results for different LLMs and adaptive drafting algorithms show that AHASD achieves up to 4.2$\times$ in throughput and 5.6$\times$ in energy efficiency improvements over a GPU-only baseline, and 1.5$\times$ in throughput and 1.24$\times$ in energy efficiency gains over the state-of-the-art GPU+PIM baseline, with hardware overhead below 3\% of the DRAM area.

ai.tldr: 提出一种面向移动设备的异步异构架构AHASD，通过任务级解耦和动态控制实现投机解码的高效推理。
ai.motivation: 移动单NPU-PIM系统上自适应投机解码面临同步执行开销大和异步执行因草稿长度波动导致计算浪费的问题。
ai.method: 采用任务级DLM-TLM解耦实现PIM并行草稿生成与NPU验证；引入熵-历史感知草稿控制和时间感知预验证控制动态管理自适应算法；集成注意力算法单元和门控任务调度单元实现亚微秒级任务切换。
ai.result: 相比纯GPU基线吞吐量提升4.2倍、能效提升5.6倍；相比最先进GPU+PIM基线吞吐量提升1.5倍、能效提升1.24倍，硬件开销低于DRAM面积的3%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解移动设备上大模型推理加速的新架构设计思路。

idea_spark.idea: 利用PIM的并行计算能力与NPU的验证能力协同，通过历史熵和时序感知动态调整草稿长度。
idea_spark.risk: 动态控制策略的实时性要求高，可能增加系统复杂度；硬件定制化程度高，通用性受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从移动端资源受限出发，启发在边缘设备上设计轻量级、自适应的推理加速方案。

zh.tldr: 提出一种面向移动设备的异步异构架构AHASD，通过任务级解耦和动态控制实现投机解码的高效推理。
zh.motivation: 移动单NPU-PIM系统上自适应投机解码面临同步执行开销大和异步执行因草稿长度波动导致计算浪费的问题。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解移动设备上大模型推理加速的新架构设计思路。

idea_spark.idea: 利用PIM的并行计算能力与NPU的验证能力协同，通过历史熵和时序感知动态调整草稿长度。
idea_spark.risk: 动态控制策略的实时性要求高，可能增加系统复杂度；硬件定制化程度高，通用性受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从移动端资源受限出发，启发在边缘设备上设计轻量级、自适应的推理加速方案。

en.tldr: Proposes AHASD, an asynchronous heterogeneous architecture for mobile devices, achieving efficient speculative decoding via task-level decoupling and dynamic control.
en.motivation: Adaptive speculative decoding on mobile single-NPU-PIM systems suffers from synchronous execution overhead and wasted computation due to draft length fluctuations in asynchronous execution.
en.method: Employs task-level DLM-TLM decoupling for parallel drafting on PIM and verification on NPU; introduces Entropy-History-Aware Drafting Control and Time-Aware Pre-Verification Control to dynamically manage adaptive algorithms; integrates Attention Algorithm Units and Gated Task Scheduling Units for sub-microsecond task switching.
en.result: Achieves up to 4.2× throughput and 5.6× energy efficiency improvements over GPU-only baseline, and 1.5× throughput and 1.24× energy efficiency gains over state-of-the-art GPU+PIM baseline, with hardware overhead below 3% of DRAM area.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand a new architecture design for accelerating large model inference on mobile devices.

idea_spark.idea: Leverages PIM parallelism and NPU verification collaboratively, dynamically adjusting draft length based on historical entropy and timing.
idea_spark.risk: Real-time requirements of dynamic control may increase system complexity; high hardware customization limits generality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by resource-constrained mobile devices, suggests designing lightweight, adaptive inference acceleration schemes for edge devices.

papers.title: MarkIt: Training-Free Visual Markers for Precise Video Temporal Grounding
papers.summary: Video temporal grounding (VTG) aims to localize the start and end timestamps of the event described by a given query within an untrimmed video. Despite the strong open-world video understanding and recognition ability of video language large models (Vid-LLMs), outputting precise temporal grounding information remains challenging, since explicit temporal cues are scarce in untrimmed videos, and query-relevant entities are hard to track consistently across the video timeline. In this paper, we present \MarkIt{}, a training-free framework that transforms an input video into a query-conditioned marked video, which empowers Vid-LLMs to generate more reliable temporal localization predictions. The core component of \MarkIt{} is an annotation-free query-to-mask grounding bridge (Q2M-Bridge). Given a natural-language query, it automatically derives a compact set of canonical subject tags through linguistic parsing and normalization, then maps these tags to query-conditioned instance masks using text-conditioned open-vocabulary segmentation. The bridge also embeds lightweight semantic instance markers and a persistent frame index into each frame, effectively transforming long-range temporal reasoning into explicit visual cues for Vid-LLMs. \MarkIt{} adopts an inference-time plug-and-play design, needs no modifications to Vid-LLM weights, and is fully compatible with supervised fine-tuning. Experiments conducted on multiple mainstream moment retrieval and highlight detection benchmarks demonstrate that \MarkIt {} achieves state-of-the-art results, delivering consistent temporal grounding improvements across a wide range of existing models.

ai.tldr: 提出无需训练的MarkIt框架，通过查询条件标记视频，提升视频语言大模型在时间定位任务上的精度。
ai.motivation: 现有视频语言大模型虽具备强开放世界理解能力，但缺乏显式时间线索，难以精确输出时间边界。
ai.method: 构建无标注的查询到掩码桥接模块，自动解析查询生成规范主体标签，映射为条件实例掩码，并在帧中嵌入语义标记和帧索引。
ai.result: 在多个主流基准上达到最先进性能，且无需修改模型权重，兼容微调。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种即插即用的推理增强方法，无需额外训练即可提升现有模型的时间定位准确性。

idea_spark.idea: 将自然语言查询转化为显式视觉标记，使大模型能直接“看到”时间线索，类似为视频添加可读的注释。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖开放词汇分割的质量，复杂场景下主体识别可能不准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类阅读时用荧光笔标记重点的启发，将关键信息直接标注在视频帧上辅助模型理解。

zh.tldr: 提出无需训练的MarkIt框架，通过查询条件标记视频，提升视频语言大模型在时间定位任务上的精度。
zh.motivation: 现有视频语言大模型虽具备强开放世界理解能力，但缺乏显式时间线索，难以精确输出时间边界。
zh.method: 构建无标注的查询到掩码桥接模块，自动解析查询生成规范主体标签，映射为条件实例掩码，并在帧中嵌入语义标记和帧索引。
zh.result: 在多个主流基准上达到最先进性能，且无需修改模型权重，兼容微调。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种即插即用的推理增强方法，无需额外训练即可提升现有模型的时间定位准确性。

idea_spark.idea: 将自然语言查询转化为显式视觉标记，使大模型能直接“看到”时间线索，类似为视频添加可读的注释。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖开放词汇分割的质量，复杂场景下主体识别可能不准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类阅读时用荧光笔标记重点的启发，将关键信息直接标注在视频帧上辅助模型理解。

en.tldr: Proposes MarkIt, a training-free framework that transforms videos into query-conditioned marked videos, improving temporal grounding precision for Vid-LLMs.
en.motivation: Existing Vid-LLMs lack explicit temporal cues, making precise timestamp localization difficult despite strong open-world understanding.
en.method: Builds an annotation-free query-to-mask grounding bridge that parses queries into canonical subject tags, maps them to instance masks via open-vocabulary segmentation, and embeds semantic markers and frame indices into frames.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks without modifying model weights, and is compatible with fine-tuning.
en.help_to_user: Provides a plug-and-play inference enhancement that boosts temporal localization accuracy of existing models without extra training.

idea_spark.idea: Convert natural language queries into explicit visual markers, allowing large models to directly 'see' temporal cues, akin to adding readable annotations to video frames.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the quality of open-vocabulary segmentation; subject identification may be inaccurate in complex scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by how humans use highlighters to mark key points while reading, directly annotating critical information on video frames to aid model comprehension.

papers.title: SciEval: A Benchmark for Automatic Evaluation of K-12 Science Instructional Materials
papers.summary: The need to evaluate instructional materials for K-12 science education has become increasingly important, as more educators use generative AI to create instructional materials. However, the review of instructional materials is time-consuming, expertise-intensive, and difficult to scale, motivating interest in automated evaluation approaches. While large language models (LLMs) have shown strong performance on general evaluation tasks, their performance and reliability on instructional materials remain unclear. To address this gap, we formulate Automatic Instructional Materials Evaluation (AIME) as a generative AI task that predicts scores and evidence using the rubric designed by the educator. We create a benchmark dataset and develop baseline models for AIME. First, we curate the first AIME dataset, SciEval, consisting of instructional materials annotated with pedagogy-aligned evaluation scores and evidence-based rationales. Expert annotations achieve high inter-rater reliability, resulting in a dataset of 273 lesson-level instructional materials evaluated across 13 criteria (N=3549) using the EQuIP rubric. Second, we test mainstream LLMs (GPT, Gemini, Llama, and Qwen) on SciEval and find that none achieve strong performance. Then we fine-tune Qwen3 on SciEval. Results on a held-out test set show that domain-aligned fine-tuning can achieve up to 11 percent performance gains, highlighting the importance of domain-specific fine-tuning for AIME and facilitating the use of LLMs in other educational tasks.

ai.tldr: 提出了SciEval基准，用于自动评估K-12科学教学材料，并发现领域微调可提升LLM性能。
ai.motivation: 教师使用生成式AI创建教学材料增多，但人工评估耗时且难以扩展，需要自动化方法。
ai.method: 构建包含273份教学材料、按EQuIP标准标注的SciEval数据集，微调Qwen3模型。
ai.result: 主流LLM在SciEval上表现不佳，领域微调后性能提升最高达11%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助教育工作者和研究者快速评估AI生成的教学材料质量。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多模态数据（如学生反馈）进一步优化评估模型。
idea_spark.risk: 领域微调可能过拟合特定标准，泛化性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM的推理能力自动生成评估证据，减少人工标注成本。

zh.tldr: 提出了SciEval基准，用于自动评估K-12科学教学材料，并发现领域微调可提升LLM性能。

papers.title: Lightweight Real-Time Rendering Parameter Optimization via XGBoost-Driven Lookup Tables
papers.summary: Achieving a desirable balance between rendering quality and real-time performance is a long-standing challenge in modern game and rendering engines, particularly on resource-constrained mobile devices such as laptops, tablets, and smartphones. Existing approaches to automatic rendering parameter optimization either depend on exhaustive per-scene pre-computation that spans several days, suffer from the prohibitive inference overhead of neural networks that prevents per-frame adaptation, or lack generalizability across heterogeneous hardware and diverse scenes. In this paper, we propose \textbf{LUT-Opt}, a lightweight, general-purpose framework for adaptive per-frame rendering parameter optimization. Our method decomposes the joint optimization of rendering time and image quality into a tractable two-stage pipeline. In the offline stage, we train a pair of XGBoost regressors to predict rendering time and image quality from rendering parameters, hardware state, and scene complexity descriptors. The trained ensemble models are then distilled into compact lookup tables (LUTs) through systematic discretization and a two-phase linear search that first constrains rendering time and subsequently maximizes structural similarity (SSIM). During runtime, the pre-computed LUT is queried every frame in sub-millisecond time, enabling truly adaptive parameter selection with negligible computational overhead. We validate LUT-Opt on two representative rendering techniques -- subsurface scattering (SSS) and hybrid-pipeline ambient occlusion (AO) -- implemented within Unreal Engine 5. Extensive experiments across multiple scenes and GPU configurations demonstrate that LUT-Opt reduces subsurface scattering rendering time by approximately 40\% and ambient occlusion rendering time by roughly 70\%, while incurring only about 2\% increase in image quality error, with per-frame inference latency below 0.1\ ms.

ai.tldr: 提出LUT-Opt框架，通过XGBoost回归器蒸馏为查找表，实现亚毫秒级逐帧渲染参数自适应优化，在保持图像质量的同时大幅降低渲染时间。
ai.motivation: 现有渲染参数优化方法要么需要数天预计算，要么神经网络推理开销大无法逐帧调整，且缺乏跨硬件和场景的泛化能力。
ai.method: 离线阶段训练XGBoost回归器预测渲染时间和图像质量，通过系统离散化和两阶段线性搜索蒸馏为紧凑查找表；运行时每帧亚毫秒查询查找表。
ai.result: 在Unreal Engine 5中，次表面散射渲染时间减少约40%，环境光遮蔽减少约70%，图像质量误差仅增加约2%，推理延迟低于0.1毫秒。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在资源受限设备上实现高质量实时渲染，无需手动调参或大量预计算。

idea_spark.idea: 将XGBoost模型蒸馏为查找表，结合两阶段搜索平衡时间和质量，实现轻量级逐帧优化。

papers.title: Categorical Symmetries via Operator Algebras
papers.summary: We propose that the symmetry category associated to a 2D quantum field theory with 0-form $G$-symmetry with 't Hooft anomaly $k\in H^4(BG,\mathbb{Z})$ for a large class of Lie groups $G$ is the category of twisted measurable fields of Hilbert spaces over $G$ denoted by $\mathrm{Hilb}^k(G)$, which is equivalent to the category of unitary representations of $C_0(G)$ with convolution product twisted by a multiplicative bundle gerbe labeled by $k$ denoted by $\textbf{Rep}^k(C_0(G))$. We find that the Drinfeld center of the symmetry category $\mathcal{Z}(\mathrm{Hilb}^{k}(G))$ equivalent to the category of unitary representations of the groupoid $C^*$-algebra of the Fell line bundle $Σ_k$ over the conjugation action groupoid $G//_{\rm Ad} G$, denoted by $\textbf{Rep}(C^*(G//_{\rm Ad}G,Σ_k))$, where the twist is characterized by the transgression $τ(k)\in H^2(G//_{\rm Ad}G,U(1))$. To the full generality, our framework applies to a Lie group $G$ that is a direct product of a compact connected Lie group and a number of $\mathbb{R}$ or $GL(1,\mathbb{C})$ factors. We compute the braiding of anyon lines in the bulk 3D SymTFT from this formalism. Finally we provide physical examples for abelian and non-abelian $G$, and discuss the physical consequences of flat gauging continuous global symmetries.

ai.tldr: 提出用算子代数描述二维量子场论中带有't Hooft反常的0-形式G对称性，并构造了对称范畴及其Drinfeld中心，计算了体态SymTFT中任意子线的编织。
ai.motivation: 为连续李群G的0-形式对称性及其反常提供统一的范畴化描述，并建立与算子代数的联系。
ai.method: 引入扭曲可测希尔伯特空间场范畴Hilb^k(G)等价于C_0(G)的扭曲卷积表示范畴，通过Fell线丛和群胚C*-代数计算Drinfeld中心。
ai.result: 对称范畴的Drinfeld中心等价于共轭作用群胚上Fell线丛的群胚C*-代数的表示范畴，编织由反常的上同调类决定。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解连续对称性及其反常的范畴化框架，以及体态SymTFT中任意子线的编织结构。

idea_spark.idea: 利用群胚C*-代数描述对称范畴的Drinfeld中心，为计算任意子编织提供代数工具。
idea_spark.risk: 对非紧致李群或更复杂反常的适用性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将连续对称性的反常编码为线丛的曲率，启发用几何工具研究对称性。

en.tldr: We propose that the symmetry category for a 2D QFT with 0-form G-symmetry and 't Hooft anomaly is the category of twisted measurable fields of Hilbert spaces over G, and compute its Drinfeld center via groupoid C*-algebras, yielding the braiding of anyon lines in the bulk SymTFT.
en.motivation: To provide a unified categorical description of 0-form symmetries and their anomalies for continuous Lie groups G, linking to operator algebras.
en.method: Introduce the category Hilb^k(G) of twisted measurable fields of Hilbert spaces, equivalent to twisted convolution representations of C_0(G), and compute the Drinfeld center using Fell line bundles and groupoid C*-algebras.
en.result: The Drinfeld center is equivalent to the representation category of the groupoid C*-algebra of the Fell line bundle over the conjugation action groupoid, with braiding determined by the transgression of the anomaly.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the categorical framework for continuous symmetries and anomalies, and the braiding structure of anyons in the bulk SymTFT.

idea_spark.idea: Use groupoid C*-algebras to describe the Drinfeld center of symmetry categories, providing algebraic tools for computing anyon braiding.
idea_spark.risk: Applicability to non-compact Lie groups or more complex anomalies needs further verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Encoding anomalies as curvature of line bundles inspires geometric methods for studying symmetries.

papers.title: GramSR: Visual Feature Conditioning for Diffusion-Based Super-Resolution
papers.summary: Despite recent advances, single-image super-resolution (SR) remains challenging, especially in real-world scenarios with complex degradations. Diffusion-based SR methods, particularly those built on Stable Diffusion, leverage strong generative priors but commonly rely on text conditioning derived from semantic captioning. Such textual descriptions provide only high-level semantics and lack the spatially aligned visual information required for faithful restoration, leading to a representation gap between abstract semantics and spatially aligned visual details. To address this limitation, we propose GramSR, a one-step diffusion-based SR framework that replaces text conditioning with dense visual features extracted from the low-resolution input using a pre-trained DINOv3 encoder. GramSR adopts a three-stage LoRA architecture, where pixel-level, semantic-level, and texture-level LoRA modules are trained sequentially. The pixel-level module focuses on degradation removal using $\ell_2$ loss, the semantic-level module enhances perceptual details via LPIPS and CSD losses, and the texture-level module enforces feature correlation consistency through a Gram matrix loss computed from DINOv3 features. At inference, independent guidance scales enable flexible control over degradation removal, semantic enhancement, and texture preservation. Extensive experiments on standard SR benchmarks demonstrate that GramSR consistently outperforms existing one-step diffusion-based methods, achieving superior structural fidelity and texture realism. The code for this work is available at: https://github.com/aimagelab/GramSR.

ai.tldr: 提出GramSR，用DINOv3视觉特征替代文本条件，通过三阶段LoRA实现一步扩散超分辨率，在结构保真度和纹理真实性上超越现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有扩散超分辨率方法依赖文本条件，缺乏空间对齐的视觉细节，导致语义与视觉信息之间存在表征鸿沟。
ai.method: 采用预训练DINOv3编码器提取低分辨率图像的密集视觉特征，替换文本条件；设计三阶段LoRA架构，依次训练像素级、语义级和纹理级模块，分别使用ℓ2损失、LPIPS/CSD损失和Gram矩阵损失。
ai.result: 在标准超分辨率基准上，GramSR在结构保真度和纹理真实性上一致优于现有一步扩散方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种无需文本描述、仅依赖视觉特征的高质量超分辨率方案，适用于复杂退化场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将DINOv3特征与其他预训练视觉编码器（如CLIP视觉分支）结合，或引入多尺度特征融合。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖预训练编码器的质量，可能对未见过的退化类型泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 三阶段LoRA训练范式可启发其他需要多目标优化的生成任务。

en.tldr: GramSR replaces text conditioning with DINOv3 visual features and uses a three-stage LoRA architecture for one-step diffusion super-resolution, achieving superior structural fidelity and texture realism.
en.motivation: Existing diffusion-based SR methods rely on text conditioning, which lacks spatially aligned visual details, causing a representation gap between semantics and visual information.
en.method: Extract dense visual features from low-resolution input using a pre-trained DINOv3 encoder to replace text conditioning; adopt a three-stage LoRA architecture with pixel-level, semantic-level, and texture-level modules trained sequentially using ℓ2, LPIPS/CSD, and Gram matrix losses.
en.result: GramSR consistently outperforms existing one-step diffusion-based methods on standard SR benchmarks in structural fidelity and texture realism.
en.help_to_user: Provides a high-quality super-resolution solution that relies solely on visual features without text descriptions, suitable for complex degradation scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining DINOv3 features with other pre-trained visual encoders (e.g., CLIP visual branch) or introducing multi-scale feature fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on the quality of the pre-trained encoder may lead to poor generalization on unseen degradation types.
idea_spark.inspiration: The three-stage LoRA training paradigm can inspire other generative tasks requiring multi-objective optimization.

papers.title: Stabilizers for Compiling Logical Circuits under Hardware Constraints
papers.summary: To implement quantum algorithms on a quantum computer, we must overcome the twin problems of fault-tolerance -- how can we realize a relatively noiseless computation by cleverly combining noisy components? -- and compilation -- how can we realize an arbitrary quantum algorithm given the basic operations available on the quantum device at hand? We show how treating the former problem via error-correcting codes enables greater flexibility in resolving the latter. Specifically, we explicitly leverage the fact that error-correcting codes introduce redundancy which renders physically distinct operators logically indistinguishable. In terms of computation, it suffices to implement any operator logically equivalent to some target, yet from a compilation perspective, certain choices may be preferable to others. Our novel contribution is making this intuition precise in the general setting of the special unitary group. In particular, we describe how to reduce the problem of making a compilation-ideal choice to a least squares problem and provide a closed form solution thereof. Using our framework, it is possible to circumvent inserting costly swaps to adhere to hardware connectivity; instead, we could realize the logical target through a distinct physical Hamiltonian that is natively accessible. We elucidate our approach using the $[[4,2,2]]$ code. We discuss connections to compressed sensing that may pave the way to efficient compilation leveraging physical degrees of freedom.

ai.tldr: 提出一种利用量子纠错码的冗余性，在硬件约束下灵活编译逻辑电路的方法，将编译优化问题转化为最小二乘问题并给出闭式解。
ai.motivation: 量子计算面临容错和编译两大难题，传统方法需插入昂贵的SWAP门以满足硬件连接性，效率低下。
ai.method: 利用纠错码引入的物理算符逻辑等价性，将选择编译最优物理算符的问题转化为最小二乘问题，并给出闭式解。
ai.result: 以[[4,2,2]]码为例，展示了无需SWAP门即可实现逻辑目标，并讨论了与压缩感知的联系。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用纠错码的冗余性来简化量子电路编译，避免硬件连接性带来的额外开销。

idea_spark.idea: 将编译优化问题转化为最小二乘问题，利用闭式解直接得到最优物理实现，避免搜索。
idea_spark.risk: 闭式解可能对噪声或近似误差敏感，实际硬件实现中需验证稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 压缩感知技术的引入可能实现高效编译，利用物理自由度降低资源消耗。
