en.tldr: This paper uncovers an exact χ² dissipation identity for the Blahut-Arimoto flow and establishes its information-geometric structure, proving that the Pearson χ² divergence is the global non-asymptotic driver of the dynamics.
en.motivation: Prior works only analyzed BA convergence via monotone decrease of KL divergence; this work aims to reveal a deeper dissipative structure.
en.method: By analyzing the continuous-time flow ∂_τq = \tilde{q}_β[q] - q, we prove an exact dissipation identity for the Pearson χ² divergence and establish a unified dissipation identity.
en.result: The χ² divergence and symmetrized Jeffreys divergence differ only by a cubic residual that vanishes exactly for BA flow; the spectral gap determines local exponential convergence rate and stability; for Gaussian sources, an exact closed ODE for reproduction variance and spectral stiffness are derived.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the convergence mechanism and geometric essence of the BA algorithm, providing new dissipative interpretations for information-theoretic optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Design new accelerated convergence algorithms or adaptive step-size strategies using the χ² dissipation identity.
idea_spark.risk: The theoretical derivation relies on the specific flow structure; generalization to discrete or non-Gaussian cases may require additional assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting classical algorithms from an information-geometric perspective reveals overlooked exact identities, inspiring geometric analysis of more optimization dynamics.

papers.title: Modeling Human-Like Color Naming Behavior in Context
papers.summary: Modeling the emergence of human-like lexicons in computational systems has advanced through the use of interacting neural agents, which simulate both learning and communicative pressures. The NeLLCom-Lex framework (Zhang et al., 2025) allows neural agents to develop pragmatic color naming behavior and human-like lexicons through supervised learning (SL) from human data and reinforcement learning (RL) in referential games. Despite these successes, the lexicons that emerge diverge systematically from human color categories, producing highly non-convex regions in color space, which contrast with the convexity typical of human categories. To address this, we introduce two factors, upsampling rare color terms during SL and multi-listener RL interactions, and adopt a convexity measure to quantify geometric coherence. We find that upsampling improves lexical diversity and system-level informativeness of the color lexicon, while many-listener setups promote more convex color categories. The combination of moderate upsampling and multiple listeners produces lexicons most similar to human systems.

ai.tldr: 通过上采样稀有颜色术语和多听众强化学习，使神经代理生成更接近人类的颜色词汇。
ai.motivation: 现有神经代理在颜色命名任务中产生的词汇区域非凸，与人类颜色类别凸性特征不符。
ai.method: 在监督学习中上采样稀有颜色术语，并在指称游戏中采用多听众强化学习，同时引入凸性度量评估几何一致性。
ai.result: 上采样提高了词汇多样性和系统信息性，多听众设置促进了凸性颜色类别，两者结合产生最接近人类的词汇。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过计算模型模拟人类颜色命名行为，并改进代理的词汇几何特性。

idea_spark.idea: 结合数据不平衡处理和交互多样性，优化代理的类别边界。
idea_spark.risk: 上采样可能引入偏差，多听众增加计算成本。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类认知的凸性偏好出发，设计更自然的代理学习机制。

idea_spark.risk: 上采样可能引入偏差，多听众增加计算成本。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类认知的凸性偏好出发，设计更自然的代理学习机制。

en.tldr: Upsampling rare color terms and multi-listener reinforcement learning make neural agents produce lexicons closer to human color categories.
en.motivation: Existing neural agents produce non-convex color regions, contrasting with the convexity typical of human categories.
en.method: Upsample rare color terms in supervised learning and adopt multi-listener reinforcement learning in referential games, using a convexity measure to quantify geometric coherence.
en.result: Upsampling improves lexical diversity and system-level informativeness; multi-listener setups promote convex categories; their combination yields lexicons most similar to human systems.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to model human-like color naming behavior computationally and improve lexical geometric properties.

idea_spark.idea: Combine data imbalance handling and interaction diversity to optimize agent category boundaries.
idea_spark.risk: Upsampling may introduce bias; multi-listener increases computational cost.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw from human cognitive preference for convexity to design more natural agent learning mechanisms.

papers.title: Towards Seamless Lunar Mosaics: Deep Radiometric Normalization for Cross-Sensor Orbital Imagery Using Chandrayaan-2 TMC Data
papers.summary: Radiometric inconsistencies remain a major challenge in generating seamless lunar mosaics from multi-mission orbital imagery due to variability in illumination geometry, sensor characteristics, and acquisition conditions. This paper presents a deep learning-based radiometric normalization framework for multi-mission lunar mosaics constructed primarily from ISRO's Chandrayaan-2 Terrain Mapping Camera (TMC) data, supplemented with auxiliary imagery from the SELENE (Kaguya) mission.   The proposed approach employs a conditional generative adversarial network (cGAN) comprising a U-Net-based generator and a PatchGAN discriminator to learn a nonlinear radiometric mapping from conventionally mosaicked lunar imagery to a photometrically consistent reference derived from LROC Wide Angle Camera (WAC) data. A patch-based training strategy with overlap-aware inference is adopted to enable scalable processing of large-area mosaics while preserving structural continuity across tile boundaries.   Quantitative evaluation using Structural Similarity Index (SSIM), Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), and Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) demonstrates consistent improvements over traditional histogram-based normalization techniques. The proposed framework achieves enhanced tonal uniformity, reduced seam artifacts, and improved structural coherence across multi-source lunar datasets.   These results highlight the effectiveness of learning-based radiometric normalization for large-scale planetary mosaicking and demonstrate its potential for generating high-fidelity lunar surface maps from heterogeneous orbital imagery.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于深度学习的辐射归一化框架，用于多任务月球镶嵌图，主要使用月船二号TMC数据，并辅以SELENE影像，通过cGAN实现色调一致。
ai.motivation: 多任务轨道影像因光照、传感器和采集条件差异导致辐射不一致，影响无缝月球镶嵌图的生成。
ai.method: 采用条件生成对抗网络（cGAN），U-Net生成器与PatchGAN判别器学习从传统镶嵌图到LROC WAC光度一致参考的非线性映射，并采用分块训练与重叠感知推理。
ai.result: 在SSIM、PSNR、RMSE指标上优于传统直方图方法，色调均匀性提升，接缝伪影减少，结构连贯性增强。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何用深度学习解决多源行星遥感影像的辐射归一化问题，适用于月球制图。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将cGAN与自监督学习结合，减少对参考影像的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 参考影像（LROC WAC）的获取可能受限，且模型泛化性需更多任务验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 分块训练与重叠推理策略可启发大规模遥感影像的深度学习处理流程。

en.tldr: Proposes a deep learning radiometric normalization framework for multi-mission lunar mosaics using Chandrayaan-2 TMC data and auxiliary SELENE imagery, employing a cGAN for tonal consistency.
en.motivation: Radiometric inconsistencies due to varying illumination, sensors, and acquisition conditions hinder seamless lunar mosaic generation from multi-mission orbital imagery.
en.method: Uses a conditional GAN with U-Net generator and PatchGAN discriminator to learn a nonlinear mapping from conventional mosaics to a photometrically consistent LROC WAC reference, with patch-based training and overlap-aware inference.
en.result: Outperforms traditional histogram-based methods in SSIM, PSNR, RMSE; achieves enhanced tonal uniformity, reduced seam artifacts, and improved structural coherence.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand deep learning solutions for radiometric normalization of multi-source planetary remote sensing imagery, applicable to lunar mapping.

idea_spark.idea: Consider combining cGAN with self-supervised learning to reduce dependence on reference imagery.
idea_spark.risk: Reference imagery (LROC WAC) may not always be available; model generalization requires validation on more missions.
idea_spark.inspiration: The patch-based training and overlap-aware inference strategy can inspire deep learning pipelines for large-scale remote sensing imagery.

papers.title: CoRE: Concept-Reasoning Expansion for Continual Brain Lesion Segmentation
papers.summary: Accurate brain lesion segmentation in MRI is vital for effective clinical diagnosis and treatment planning. Due to high annotation costs and strict data privacy regulations, universal models require employing Continual Learning (CL) to adapt to evolving clinical tasks without losing previously acquired knowledge. However, existing CL paradigms often suffer from capacity limits or redundant parameter growth, and even advanced dynamic methods rely mostly on image-perception strategies that struggle to handle the substantial pathological and multimodal heterogeneity inherent in brain imaging. To address this issue, we propose Concept-Reasoning Expansion (CoRE) framework, which establishes a joint decision-making mechanism by integrating visual features with structured concepts. Through the alignment of image tokens with a hierarchical concept library, CoRE simulates clinical reasoning to guide both interpretable expert routing and demand-based model growth. This collaborative process ensures model evolution is grounded in clinical priors, preventing redundant parameter expansion while maximizing knowledge reuse. Extensive evaluations across 12 sequential brain lesion MRI tasks demonstrate that CoRE achieves state-of-the-art performance and provides a high knowledge starting point for efficient future adaptation. Its superior few-shot transferability and clinical interpretability further validate its effectiveness in managing non-stationary clinical data streams. Our code will be released soon.

ai.tldr: 提出CoRE框架，通过概念-推理扩展实现持续脑病变分割，在12个序列任务上达到最优性能。
ai.motivation: 现有持续学习方法在脑病变分割中面临容量限制或参数冗余，且难以处理病理和多模态异质性。
ai.method: 构建层次化概念库，将图像令牌与概念对齐，模拟临床推理指导专家路由和模型增长。
ai.result: 在12个序列任务上取得最优性能，具备高知识起点和少样本迁移能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用临床先验知识实现持续学习，减少冗余参数并提升可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 将临床推理过程显式建模为概念层次结构，指导模型动态扩展。
idea_spark.risk: 概念库构建依赖专家知识，可能引入主观偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从临床诊断流程中抽象出结构化概念，用于引导模型学习与决策。

en.tldr: Proposes CoRE framework for continual brain lesion segmentation via concept-reasoning expansion, achieving state-of-the-art on 12 sequential tasks.
en.motivation: Existing continual learning methods suffer from capacity limits or redundant parameters and struggle with pathological and multimodal heterogeneity in brain imaging.
en.method: Builds a hierarchical concept library, aligns image tokens with concepts, and simulates clinical reasoning to guide expert routing and model growth.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on 12 sequential tasks with high knowledge starting point and few-shot transferability.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage clinical priors for continual learning, reducing redundant parameters and improving interpretability.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly model clinical reasoning as a concept hierarchy to guide dynamic model expansion.
idea_spark.risk: Concept library construction relies on expert knowledge, which may introduce subjective bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Abstract structured concepts from clinical diagnostic workflows to guide model learning and decision-making.

papers.title: On the degradations of Binary-Input Discrete Memoryless Channels
papers.summary: For the polar codes introduced by Arikan in 2009, the first code family achieving the capacity of binary-input discrete memoryless channels (BIDMCs) with low-complexity encoding and decoding, it is crucial to evaluate the reliability of the synthetic channels resulted in the code construction. Since the synthetic channels have an output alphabet that grows exponentially with the code length, an effective method for faithfully evaluating their reliability is to replace them with degradations of manageable alphabet size. The main aim of this paper is to find the optimal degradations of symmetric BIDMCs. We determine all the degradations with the minimum probability of error decoding and give a necessary condition for the degradations with the maximum symmetric capacity. Finally, based on this necessary condition, we propose an efficient algorithm for finding degradation schemes that maximize the symmetric capacity.

ai.tldr: 本文研究对称二进制输入离散无记忆信道的最优退化问题，提出了最大化对称容量的退化方案算法。
ai.motivation: 极化码构造中合成信道的输出字母表随码长指数增长，需要可靠的退化方法来评估其可靠性。
ai.method: 确定最小译码错误概率的所有退化，给出最大对称容量的必要条件，并基于此提出高效算法。
ai.result: 找到了对称BIDMC的最优退化，并设计了最大化对称容量的退化方案算法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解极化码构造中退化方法的选择，提升信道可靠性评估的准确性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用对称容量必要条件设计退化，可能启发更高效的极化码构造算法。
idea_spark.risk: 退化方案可能引入近似误差，影响实际性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将退化问题转化为优化问题，类似思路可用于其他信息论问题。

papers.title: Prime-Field PINI: Machine-Checked Composition Theorems for Post-Quantum NTT Masking
papers.summary: This is Paper 6 of a series of formally-verified analyses of masked NTT hardware for post-quantum cryptography; Paper 1 [1] established structural dependency analysis of the QANARY platform, and Paper 2 [2] quantified security margins under partial NTT masking. Boolean masking composition is well-understood through NI, SNI, and PINI. Arithmetic masking over $\mathbb{Z}_q$ for prime $q$, the foundation of NTT-based post-quantum cryptography, has lacked an analogous theory. We prove, to our knowledge, the first machine-checked composition theorems for arithmetic masking over prime fields. Our key insight is the renewal argument: when a fresh random mask is applied between two pipeline stages, the intermediate wire becomes perfectly uniform regardless of Stage 1's security parameter. For two PF-PINI gadgets with parameters $k_1$ and $k_2$, the composed two-stage pipeline with fresh masking satisfies PF-PINI($k_2$), Stage 1's multiplicity is completely erased from the composed output. Without fresh masking, intermediate wires have multiplicity up to $k_1$, creating a necessary condition for differential power analysis. We formalize both theorems in Lean 4 with 18 machine-checked proofs and zero sorry stubs. We formally bridge the algebraic and hardware-faithful arithmetic models of Barrett reduction, and instantiate the theorems to formally diagnose Microsoft's Adams Bridge PQC accelerator: its absence of fresh inter-stage masking leaves Barrett output wires non-uniform under the first-order probing model, the same architectural flaw that two independent empirical analyses [3, 4] and our own prior structural analysis [1] identified. Computational evidence further suggests the 1-Bit Barrier is universal across Barrett and Montgomery reductions.

ai.tldr: 本文首次为素数域上的算术掩码证明了机器检查的组合定理，填补了NTT后量子密码学中布尔掩码理论之外的空白。
ai.motivation: 布尔掩码的NI/SNI/PINI组合理论已成熟，但素数域算术掩码缺乏类似理论，阻碍了NTT后量子密码硬件的形式化安全验证。
ai.method: 提出“更新论证”：在流水线阶段间施加新鲜随机掩码，使中间线完全均匀；在Lean 4中形式化两个组合定理，共18个机器检查证明，零sorry存根。
ai.result: 两阶段PF-PINI流水线在新鲜掩码下满足PF-PINI(k2)，阶段1的阶数被完全擦除；无新鲜掩码时中间线阶数可达k1，构成DPA必要条件。形式化诊断Microsoft Adams Bridge加速器缺少级间新鲜掩码的漏洞。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助后量子密码硬件设计者理解并验证算术掩码组合的安全性，避免因缺少新鲜掩码导致的侧信道攻击。

idea_spark.idea: 将形式化组合定理集成到硬件综合工具中，自动检测流水线级间新鲜掩码缺失。
idea_spark.risk: 形式化证明依赖特定约简模型，实际硬件实现可能引入未建模的泄漏。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从布尔掩码的PINI理论出发，通过“更新”概念跨越到算术域，启发其他代数结构的掩码组合形式化。

en.tldr: This paper proves the first machine-checked composition theorems for arithmetic masking over prime fields, filling a gap in NTT-based post-quantum cryptography. The key renewal argument shows that fresh inter-stage masking erases the security parameter of the first stage. Formalized in Lean 4 with 18 proofs, the theorems diagnose a missing fresh-masking flaw in Microsoft's Adams Bridge accelerator.
en.motivation: Boolean masking composition (NI/SNI/PINI) is well understood, but arithmetic masking over prime fields lacks an analogous theory, hindering formal security verification of NTT-based post-quantum hardware.
en.method: Introduces the renewal argument: applying a fresh random mask between pipeline stages makes the intermediate wire perfectly uniform. Two composition theorems are formalized in Lean 4 with 18 machine-checked proofs and zero sorry stubs.
en.result: A two-stage PF-PINI pipeline with fresh masking satisfies PF-PINI(k2), completely erasing Stage 1's multiplicity. Without fresh masking, intermediate wires have multiplicity up to k1, a necessary condition for DPA. The theorems formally diagnose the missing inter-stage fresh masking in Microsoft's Adams Bridge accelerator.
en.help_to_user: Helps post-quantum hardware designers understand and verify the security of arithmetic masking compositions, preventing side-channel attacks due to missing fresh masks.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate the formal composition theorems into hardware synthesis tools to automatically detect missing inter-stage fresh masks.
idea_spark.risk: Formal proofs rely on specific reduction models; actual hardware implementations may introduce unmodeled leakage.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extending the PINI concept from Boolean masking to arithmetic domains via the renewal argument inspires formalization of masking composition for other algebraic structures.

papers.title: Diagnosis, Bad Planning & Reasoning. Treatment, SCOPE -- Planning for Hybrid Querying over Clinical Trial Data
papers.summary: We study clinical trial table reasoning, where answers are not directly stored in visible cells but must be reasoned from semantic understanding through normalization, classification, extraction, or lightweight domain reasoning. Motivated by the observation that current LLM approaches often suffer from "bad reasoning" under implicit planning assumptions, we focus on settings in which the model must recover implicit attributes such as therapy type, added agents, endpoint roles, or follow-up status from partially observed clinical-trial tables. We propose SCOPE (Structured Clinical hybrid Planning for Evidence retrieval in clinical trials), a multi-LLM planner-based framework that decomposes the task into row selection, structured planning, and execution. The planner makes the source field, reasoning rules, and output constraints explicit before answer generation, reducing ambiguity relative to direct prompting. We evaluate SCOPE on 1,500 hybrid reasoning questions over oncology clinical-trial tables against zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought, TableGPT2, Blend-SQL, and EHRAgent. Results show that explicit multi-LLM planning improves accuracy for reasoning-based questions while offering a stronger accuracy-efficiency tradeoff than heavier agentic baselines. Our findings position clinical trial reasoning as a distinct table understanding problem and highlight hybrid planner-based decomposition as an effective solution

ai.tldr: 提出SCOPE框架，通过多LLM规划器分解临床表格推理任务，提升隐式属性恢复的准确性。
ai.motivation: 当前LLM在临床表格推理中常因隐式规划假设导致错误推理，需显式规划以减少歧义。
ai.method: SCOPE框架将任务分解为行选择、结构化规划和执行，由规划器显式指定源字段、推理规则和输出约束。
ai.result: 在1500个肿瘤临床试验混合推理问题上，SCOPE比零样本、少样本、思维链等方法更准确，且效率优于重代理基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过显式规划提升LLM在结构化表格上的推理可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 将隐式推理规则显式化为可执行的规划步骤，减少LLM自由生成带来的不确定性。
idea_spark.risk: 多LLM协作可能增加系统复杂性和延迟，需平衡准确性与效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受软件工程中结构化编程启发，将复杂推理任务分解为明确步骤。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受软件工程中结构化编程启发，将复杂推理任务分解为明确步骤。

en.tldr: Proposes SCOPE, a multi-LLM planner framework that decomposes clinical table reasoning into explicit steps, improving accuracy for implicit attribute recovery.
en.motivation: Current LLMs suffer from bad reasoning due to implicit planning assumptions in clinical table reasoning; explicit planning reduces ambiguity.
en.method: SCOPE decomposes the task into row selection, structured planning, and execution, with the planner making source fields, reasoning rules, and output constraints explicit.
en.result: On 1,500 hybrid reasoning questions over oncology clinical trial tables, SCOPE outperforms zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought, and other baselines in accuracy while offering a better accuracy-efficiency tradeoff than heavier agentic baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how explicit planning can improve LLM reasoning reliability on structured tables.

idea_spark.idea: Make implicit reasoning rules explicit as executable planning steps to reduce uncertainty from free-form LLM generation.
idea_spark.risk: Multi-LLM collaboration may increase system complexity and latency; accuracy-efficiency tradeoff must be managed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by structured programming in software engineering, decomposing complex reasoning tasks into clear steps.

papers.title: Dynamic Decision Learning: Test-Time Evolution for Abnormality Grounding in Rare Diseases
papers.summary: Clinical abnormality grounding for rare diseases is often hindered by data scarcity, making supervised fine-tuning impractical and single-pass inference highly unstable. We propose Dynamic Decision Learning (DDL), a framework that enables frozen large vision-language models (LVLMs) to refine their decisions across both language and visual spaces by optimizing instructions and consolidating predictions under visual perturbations. This process improves localization quality and produces a consensus-based reliability score that quantifies model confidence. Results on brain imaging benchmarks, including a rare-disease dataset with 281 pathology types across models ranging from 3B to 72B parameters, show that DDL improves mAP@75 by up to 105% on rare-disease cases and outperforms adaptation baselines and supervised fine-tuning. Furthermore, DDL demonstrates stronger calibration between reliability scores and localization accuracy under severe distribution shifts and increasing task difficulty. Code is available at: https://lijunrio.github.io/DDL/

ai.tldr: 提出动态决策学习框架，使冻结的大型视觉语言模型在测试时通过优化指令和视觉扰动下的预测整合来改进异常定位，在罕见病数据集上mAP@75提升高达105%。
ai.motivation: 罕见病的临床异常标注面临数据稀缺，监督微调不可行，单次推理极不稳定。
ai.method: 在语言和视觉空间同时优化指令并整合视觉扰动下的预测，生成基于共识的可靠性分数。
ai.result: 在包含281种病理类型的罕见病数据集上，mAP@75提升高达105%，优于适应基线和监督微调，且可靠性分数与定位精度校准更佳。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在不需额外训练数据的情况下，提升罕见病影像中异常定位的准确性和置信度评估。

idea_spark.idea: 结合视觉扰动和指令优化，使冻结模型在测试时自适应，避免微调过拟合。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖预训练LVLM的泛化能力，若模型本身对罕见病特征不敏感，效果可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用多视角预测的共识作为置信度指标，可启发其他需要可靠不确定性估计的少样本学习场景。

en.tldr: Proposes Dynamic Decision Learning, a test-time framework that refines frozen large vision-language models via instruction optimization and prediction consolidation under visual perturbations, achieving up to 105% mAP@75 improvement on rare-disease abnormality grounding.
en.motivation: Clinical abnormality grounding for rare diseases suffers from data scarcity, making supervised fine-tuning impractical and single-pass inference unstable.
en.method: Optimizes instructions and consolidates predictions under visual perturbations across both language and visual spaces, producing a consensus-based reliability score.
en.result: On a rare-disease dataset with 281 pathology types, DDL improves mAP@75 by up to 105%, outperforms adaptation baselines and supervised fine-tuning, and shows stronger calibration between reliability scores and localization accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Helps users improve abnormality localization accuracy and confidence estimation in rare-disease imaging without additional training data.

idea_spark.idea: Combines visual perturbations and instruction optimization to adapt frozen models at test time, avoiding fine-tuning overfitting.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the generalization of pre-trained LVLMs; if the model is insensitive to rare-disease features, performance may be limited.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using consensus from multi-view predictions as a confidence metric can inspire other few-shot learning scenarios requiring reliable uncertainty estimation.

papers.title: Hyperstatistics
papers.summary: We propose a general approach, named by us hyperstatistics, to treat complex systems, in which Boltzmann-Gibbs statistics breaks down in domains of the system. Hyperstatistics preserves the concavity of nonadditive $q$-entropy. We obtain analytical closed-form expressions for the here proposed $q$-generalized Boltzmann factor $B_q$ considering uniform, $γ$, Log-normal, F, and the $q$-$γ$ probability distribution functions. Remarkably, for all investigated distribution functions, $B_q$ reduces to a $q$-exponential-type function. To demonstrate the applicability of hyperstatistics, we use a table top experiment of the discharge of a capacitor considering $γ$-distributed relaxation times, the pressure decay over time associated with the pumping of $^4$He lines of a closed cycle cryostat, midrapidity data for $p$-Pb collisions at the LHC, as well as data set for acceleration distribution in turbulent systems. Furthermore, we deduce the power-law-like dielectric response using the $q$-$γ$-distribution function. Our proposal is applicable to systems with inherent non-Boltzmann-Gibbsian statistics in domains of the system.

ai.tldr: 提出超统计方法处理复杂系统中玻尔兹曼-吉布斯统计失效的情况，推导出q-广义玻尔兹曼因子，并通过多个实验验证其适用性。
ai.motivation: 标准玻尔兹曼-吉布斯统计在复杂系统局部区域失效，需要更通用的统计框架。
ai.method: 引入超统计概念，保持非可加q-熵的凹性，推导均匀、γ、对数正态、F和q-γ分布下的q-广义玻尔兹曼因子。
ai.result: 所有分布函数下玻尔兹曼因子均简化为q-指数型函数，实验数据（电容放电、氦压衰减、LHC碰撞、湍流加速）验证了模型。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理非玻尔兹曼-吉布斯统计的复杂系统提供理论工具和实验验证。

idea_spark.idea: 将q-γ分布用于介电响应建模，可能推广至其他弛豫过程。
idea_spark.risk: 分布函数选择依赖先验知识，可能限制普适性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从电容放电和低温实验数据中提炼统计规律，启发跨学科应用。

zh.result: 所有分布函数下玻尔兹曼因子均简化为q-指数型函数，实验数据（电容放电、氦压衰减、LHC碰撞、湍流加速）验证了模型。
zh.help_to_user: 为处理非玻尔兹曼-吉布斯统计的复杂系统提供理论工具和实验验证。

idea_spark.idea: 将q-γ分布用于介电响应建模，可能推广至其他弛豫过程。
idea_spark.risk: 分布函数选择依赖先验知识，可能限制普适性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从电容放电和低温实验数据中提炼统计规律，启发跨学科应用。

en.tldr: Proposes hyperstatistics to treat complex systems where Boltzmann-Gibbs statistics fails, derives q-generalized Boltzmann factors, and validates with multiple experiments.
en.motivation: Standard Boltzmann-Gibbs statistics breaks down in local domains of complex systems, requiring a more general framework.
en.method: Introduces hyperstatistics preserving concavity of nonadditive q-entropy, derives closed-form q-generalized Boltzmann factors for uniform, gamma, log-normal, F, and q-gamma distributions.
en.result: All Boltzmann factors reduce to q-exponential-type functions; validated by capacitor discharge, helium pressure decay, LHC p-Pb collisions, and turbulent acceleration data.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical tools and experimental validation for systems with non-Boltzmann-Gibbs statistics.

idea_spark.idea: Applying q-gamma distribution to dielectric response modeling may extend to other relaxation processes.
idea_spark.risk: Choice of distribution functions relies on prior knowledge, potentially limiting universality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Statistical patterns from capacitor discharge and cryostat experiments inspire cross-disciplinary applications.

papers.title: PI-TTA: Physics-Informed Source-Free Test-Time Adaptation for Robust Human Activity Recognition on Mobile Devices
papers.summary: Source-free test-time adaptation (TTA) is appealing for mobile and wearable sensing because it enables on-device personalization from unlabeled test streams without centralizing private data. However, sensor-based human activity recognition (HAR) poses challenges that are less pronounced in standard vision benchmarks: behavioral inertial streams are temporally correlated and often exhibit within-session shifts caused by sensor rotation, placement change, and sampling-rate drift. Under this streaming non-i.i.d. setting, widely used vision-style TTA objectives can become unstable, leading to overconfident errors, representation collapse, and catastrophic forgetting. We propose PI-TTA, a lightweight source-free adaptation framework that stabilizes online updates through three physics-consistent constraints: gravity consistency, short-horizon temporal continuity, and spectral stability. PI-TTA updates the same small parameter subset as strong source-free baselines and incurs only modest overhead, making it suitable for on-device deployment. Experiments on USCHAD, PAMAP2, and mHealth under long-sequence stress tests and factorized shift protocols show that PI-TTA mitigates the severe degradation observed in confidence-driven baselines and preserves stable adaptation under sustained streaming conditions. It improves long-sequence accuracy by up to 9.13% and reduces physical-violation rates by 27.5%, 24.1%, and 45.4% on USCHAD, PAMAP2, and mHealth, respectively. These results demonstrate that physics-informed adaptation can improve accuracy, stability, and deployment reliability for real-world mobile sensing systems.

ai.tldr: 提出PI-TTA，一种基于物理约束的源自由测试时自适应框架，用于移动设备上鲁棒的人体活动识别。
ai.motivation: 现有视觉领域的测试时自适应方法在传感器数据流中不稳定，易产生过自信错误、表示崩溃和灾难性遗忘。
ai.method: 通过重力一致性、短时时间连续性和频谱稳定性三个物理约束稳定在线更新，仅更新少量参数子集。
ai.result: 在USCHAD、PAMAP2和mHealth数据集上，长序列准确率提升最高9.13%，物理违反率降低27.5%至45.4%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助移动设备在无标签数据流中实现个性化自适应，提升活动识别稳定性和部署可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理先验与轻量级在线学习，避免数据集中化，保护隐私。
idea_spark.risk: 物理约束可能不适用于所有传感器场景，需验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从传感器物理特性出发设计约束，而非依赖数据统计假设。

en.tldr: Proposes PI-TTA, a physics-informed source-free test-time adaptation framework for robust human activity recognition on mobile devices.
en.motivation: Vision-style TTA methods are unstable on sensor data streams, causing overconfident errors, representation collapse, and catastrophic forgetting.
en.method: Stabilizes online updates via three physics-consistent constraints: gravity consistency, short-horizon temporal continuity, and spectral stability, updating only a small parameter subset.
en.result: On USCHAD, PAMAP2, and mHealth, improves long-sequence accuracy by up to 9.13% and reduces physical-violation rates by 27.5% to 45.4%.
en.help_to_user: Enables on-device personalization from unlabeled test streams, improving activity recognition stability and deployment reliability.

idea_spark.idea: Combine physical priors with lightweight online learning to avoid data centralization and preserve privacy.
idea_spark.risk: Physical constraints may not generalize to all sensor scenarios; requires validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Design constraints based on sensor physics rather than data statistics.

papers.title: Marco-MoE: Open Multilingual Mixture-of-Expert Language Models with Efficient Upcycling
papers.summary: We present Marco-MoE, a suite of fully open multilingual sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models. Marco-MoE features a highly sparse design in which only around 5\% of the total parameters are activated per input token. This extreme sparsity, combined with upcycling from dense models, enables efficient pre-training on 5T tokens. Our models surpass similarly-sized competitors on English and multilingual benchmarks, achieving a best-in-class performance-to-compute ratio. We further post-train these models to create Marco-MoE-\textsc{Instruct} variants, which surpass the performance of competing models possessing $3$--$14\times$ more activated parameters. Our analysis reveals that Marco-MoE learns structured expert activation patterns shared across related languages, while maintaining highly specialized utilization for linguistically isolated ones. We further show that Marco-MoE allows for scalable language expansion without the interference typical of dense models. To support the community, we disclose our full training datasets, recipes, and model weights.

ai.tldr: 提出Marco-MoE，一套完全开源的多语言稀疏混合专家模型，仅激活约5%参数，通过从稠密模型升级实现高效预训练，在英语和多语言基准上超越同类模型。
ai.motivation: 现有稠密模型在多语言扩展时存在干扰，且计算效率低；稀疏MoE模型虽能提升效率，但缺乏完全开源的多语言版本。
ai.method: 采用高度稀疏设计（每token仅激活5%参数），从稠密模型进行高效升级（upcycling），在5T token上预训练，并进一步后训练得到指令微调版本。
ai.result: Marco-MoE在英语和多语言基准上超越同等规模竞争者，指令微调版本以更少激活参数超越3-14倍参数模型；模型学习到结构化专家激活模式，支持可扩展语言扩展。
ai.help_to_user: 提供完全开源的训练数据、配方和模型权重，便于研究者复现和定制多语言MoE模型。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将结构化专家激活模式用于跨语言知识迁移，或利用稀疏性实现动态语言路由。
idea_spark.risk: 极端稀疏可能导致某些语言专家利用率不足，需平衡稀疏度与模型容量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从稠密模型升级到稀疏MoE的思路可启发其他模型架构的高效扩展方法。

zh.tldr: 提出Marco-MoE，一套完全开源的多语言稀疏混合专家模型，仅激活约5%参数，通过从稠密模型升级实现高效预训练，在英语和多语言基准上超越同类模型。
zh.motivation: 现有稠密模型在多语言扩展时存在干扰，且计算效率低；稀疏MoE模型虽能提升效率，但缺乏完全开源的多语言版本。
zh.method: 采用高度稀疏设计（每token仅激活5%参数），从稠密模型进行高效升级（upcycling），在5T token上预训练，并进一步后训练得到指令微调版本。
zh.result: Marco-MoE在英语和多语言基准上超越同等规模竞争者，指令微调版本以更少激活参数超越3-14倍参数模型；模型学习到结构化专家激活模式，支持可扩展语言扩展。
zh.help_to_user: 提供完全开源的训练数据、配方和模型权重，便于研究者复现和定制多语言MoE模型。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将结构化专家激活模式用于跨语言知识迁移，或利用稀疏性实现动态语言路由。
idea_spark.risk: 极端稀疏可能导致某些语言专家利用率不足，需平衡稀疏度与模型容量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从稠密模型升级到稀疏MoE的思路可启发其他模型架构的高效扩展方法。

en.tldr: We present Marco-MoE, a fully open multilingual sparse Mixture-of-Experts model that activates only ~5% of parameters per token, achieves efficient pre-training via upcycling from dense models, and outperforms similarly-sized competitors on English and multilingual benchmarks.
en.motivation: Dense models suffer from interference during language expansion and low computational efficiency; sparse MoE models improve efficiency but lack fully open multilingual versions.
en.method: We adopt a highly sparse design (5% activated parameters per token), upcycle from dense models, pre-train on 5T tokens, and further post-train to create Instruct variants.
en.result: Marco-MoE surpasses similarly-sized competitors on English and multilingual benchmarks; Instruct variants outperform models with 3-14x more activated parameters. The model learns structured expert activation patterns and supports scalable language expansion.
en.help_to_user: We release full training datasets, recipes, and model weights to support community reproduction and customization.

idea_spark.idea: Explore using structured expert activation patterns for cross-lingual knowledge transfer, or leverage sparsity for dynamic language routing.
idea_spark.risk: Extreme sparsity may lead to underutilization of experts for some languages; balance between sparsity and model capacity is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: The idea of upcycling from dense to sparse MoE can inspire efficient scaling methods for other model architectures.

papers.title: From Syntax to Emotion: A Mechanistic Analysis of Emotion Inference in LLMs
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in emotionally sensitive human-AI applications, yet little is known about how emotion recognition is internally represented. In this work, we investigate the internal mechanisms of emotion recognition in LLMs using sparse autoencoders (SAEs). By analyzing sparse feature activations across layers, we identify a consistent three-phase information flow, in which emotion-related features emerge only in the final phase. We further show that emotion representations comprise both shared features across emotions and emotion-specific features. Using phase-stratified causal tracing, we identify a small set of features that strongly influence emotion predictions, and show that both their number and causal impact vary across emotions; in particular, Disgust is more weakly and diffusely represented than other emotions. Finally, we propose an interpretable and data-efficient causal feature steering method that significantly improves emotion recognition performance across multiple models while largely preserving language modeling ability, and demonstrate that these improvements generalize across multiple emotion recognition datasets. Overall, our findings provide a systematic analysis of the internal mechanisms underlying emotion recognition in LLMs and introduce an efficient, interpretable, and controllable approach for improving model performance.

ai.tldr: 本文通过稀疏自编码器分析LLM中情感识别的内部机制，发现情感特征仅在最后阶段出现，并提出一种因果特征引导方法提升情感识别性能。
ai.motivation: LLM在情感敏感应用中广泛使用，但情感识别的内部表示机制尚不明确。
ai.method: 使用稀疏自编码器分析层间特征激活，结合分层因果追踪识别关键特征，并设计因果特征引导方法。
ai.result: 情感表示包含共享和特定特征；厌恶情感表示较弱且分散；引导方法显著提升情感识别性能，同时保持语言建模能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解LLM情感推理的内部机制，并提供一种可解释、高效的情感识别改进方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用稀疏自编码器定位关键特征，通过因果干预实现可控的情感识别增强。
idea_spark.risk: 引导方法可能引入偏见或过度依赖特定特征，需验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将情感推理分解为多阶段信息流，启发设计更透明的AI情感系统。

idea_spark.risk: 引导方法可能引入偏见或过度依赖特定特征，需验证泛化性。

en.tldr: This work uses sparse autoencoders to analyze the internal mechanisms of emotion recognition in LLMs, revealing that emotion-related features emerge only in the final phase, and proposes a causal feature steering method that improves emotion recognition performance while preserving language modeling ability.
en.motivation: LLMs are increasingly used in emotionally sensitive applications, yet the internal representation of emotion recognition remains poorly understood.
en.method: Sparse autoencoders are applied to analyze feature activations across layers, phase-stratified causal tracing identifies key features, and a causal feature steering method is designed for interpretable improvement.
en.result: Emotion representations include both shared and emotion-specific features; Disgust is more weakly and diffusely represented; the steering method significantly improves emotion recognition across multiple models and datasets.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic understanding of how LLMs infer emotions internally and offers an efficient, interpretable approach to enhance emotion recognition.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage sparse autoencoders to pinpoint critical features and use causal intervention for controllable emotion recognition enhancement.
idea_spark.risk: Steering may introduce biases or over-reliance on specific features; generalization needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Decomposing emotion inference into multi-phase information flow inspires the design of more transparent AI emotion systems.

papers.title: Libra-VLA: Achieving Learning Equilibrium via Asynchronous Coarse-to-Fine Dual-System
papers.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are a promising paradigm for generalist robotic manipulation by grounding high-level semantic instructions into executable physical actions. However, prevailing approaches typically adopt a monolithic generation paradigm, directly mapping visual-linguistic features to high-frequency motor commands in a flat, non-hierarchical fashion. This strategy overlooks the inherent hierarchy of robotic manipulation, where complex actions can be naturally modeled in a Hybrid Action Space, decomposing into discrete macro-directional reaching and continuous micro-pose alignment, severely widening the semantic-actuation gap and imposing a heavy representational burden on grounding high-level semantics to continuous actions. To address this, we introduce Libra-VLA, a novel Coarse-to-Fine Dual-System VLA architecture. We explicitly decouple the learning complexity into a coarse-to-fine hierarchy to strike a training equilibrium, while simultaneously leveraging this structural modularity to implement an asynchronous execution strategy. The Semantic Planner predicts discrete action tokens capturing macro-directional intent, while the Action Refiner conditions on coarse intent to generate high-frequency continuous actions for precise alignment. Crucially, our empirical analysis reveals that performance follows an inverted-U curve relative to action decomposition granularity, peaking exactly when the learning difficulty is balanced between the two sub-systems. With the asynchronous design, our approach offers a scalable, robust, and responsive solution for open-world manipulation.

ai.tldr: 提出Libra-VLA，通过异步粗到细双系统架构平衡学习难度，实现开放世界机器人操作。
ai.motivation: 现有VLA模型采用扁平化生成范式，忽略操作层次性，导致语义-动作鸿沟大、表示负担重。
ai.method: 将动作空间分解为离散宏方向与连续微姿态，分别由语义规划器和动作精炼器异步处理，并基于倒U曲线平衡两系统学习难度。
ai.result: 性能在动作分解粒度平衡时达到峰值，异步设计提供可扩展、鲁棒、响应的解决方案。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过层次化异步架构提升VLA模型在复杂操作任务中的效果。

idea_spark.idea: 探索动态调整动作分解粒度以自适应不同任务难度。
idea_spark.risk: 异步执行可能引入时序不一致，需设计同步机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 倒U曲线启示：系统间学习难度平衡是性能关键，可推广至多模态学习。

en.tldr: Libra-VLA achieves learning equilibrium via an asynchronous coarse-to-fine dual-system architecture for open-world robotic manipulation.
en.motivation: Existing VLA models adopt a flat generation paradigm, overlooking the hierarchy of manipulation, leading to a wide semantic-actuation gap and heavy representational burden.
en.method: Decomposes action space into discrete macro-direction and continuous micro-pose, processed asynchronously by a Semantic Planner and Action Refiner, with learning difficulty balanced via an inverted-U curve.
en.result: Performance peaks when action decomposition granularity balances learning difficulty; asynchronous design yields scalable, robust, and responsive solutions.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how hierarchical asynchronous architectures improve VLA models for complex manipulation tasks.

idea_spark.risk: Asynchronous execution may introduce temporal inconsistency; synchronization mechanisms are needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: The inverted-U curve suggests that balancing learning difficulty between subsystems is key to performance, applicable to multimodal learning.

papers.title: Luminol-AIDetect: Fast Zero-shot Machine-Generated Text Detection based on Perplexity under Text Shuffling
papers.summary: Machine-generated text (MGT) detection requires identifying structurally invariant signals across generation models, rather than relying on model-specific fingerprints. In this respect, we hypothesize that while large language models excel at local semantic consistency, their autoregressive nature results in a specific kind of structural fragility compared to human writing. We propose Luminol-AIDetect, a novel, zero-shot statistical approach that exposes this fragility through coherence disruption. By applying a simple randomized text-shuffling procedure, we demonstrate that the resulting shift in perplexity serves as a principled, model-agnostic discriminant, as MGT displays a characteristic dispersion in perplexity-under-shuffling that differs markedly from the more stable structural variability of human-written text. Luminol-AIDetect leverages this distinction to inform its decision process, where a handful of perplexity-based scalar features are extracted from an input text and its shuffled version, then detection is performed via density estimation and ensemble-based prediction. Evaluated across 8 content domains, 11 adversarial attack types, and 18 languages, Luminol-AIDetect demonstrates state-of-the-art performance, with gains up to 17x lower FPR while being cheaper than prior methods.

ai.tldr: 提出Luminol-AIDetect，一种基于文本打乱后困惑度变化的零样本机器生成文本检测方法。
ai.motivation: 现有检测方法依赖模型特定指纹，难以泛化；而人类与机器文本在结构稳定性上存在本质差异。
ai.method: 对输入文本进行随机打乱，提取打乱前后的困惑度标量特征，通过密度估计和集成预测进行检测。
ai.result: 在8个内容领域、11种对抗攻击和18种语言上达到最优性能，假阳性率降低最多17倍，且计算成本更低。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速、准确地识别机器生成的文本，无需预训练或模型特定知识。

idea_spark.idea: 利用文本打乱破坏局部连贯性，通过困惑度变化幅度区分机器与人类文本。
idea_spark.risk: 对高度模仿人类写作的模型可能失效，且打乱策略需针对不同语言调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从语言模型的本质脆弱性出发，设计简单而通用的检测信号。

zh.motivation: 现有检测方法依赖模型特定指纹，难以泛化；而人类与机器文本在结构稳定性上存在本质差异。

zh.result: 在8个内容领域、11种对抗攻击和18种语言上达到最优性能，假阳性率降低最多17倍，且计算成本更低。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速、准确地识别机器生成的文本，无需预训练或模型特定知识。

idea_spark.risk: 对高度模仿人类写作的模型可能失效，且打乱策略需针对不同语言调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从语言模型的本质脆弱性出发，设计简单而通用的检测信号。

en.tldr: Proposes Luminol-AIDetect, a zero-shot method for detecting machine-generated text by measuring perplexity shift after random shuffling.
en.motivation: Existing detectors rely on model-specific fingerprints and lack generalization; human and machine text differ in structural stability.
en.method: Randomly shuffle input text, extract perplexity-based scalar features from original and shuffled versions, then classify via density estimation and ensemble prediction.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance across 8 domains, 11 attack types, and 18 languages, with up to 17x lower FPR and lower cost.
en.help_to_user: Enables fast and accurate detection of machine-generated text without training or model-specific knowledge.

idea_spark.idea: Exploit text shuffling to disrupt local coherence and use perplexity variation as a discriminative signal.
idea_spark.risk: May fail against models that closely mimic human writing; shuffling strategy may need tuning per language.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage the inherent structural weakness of autoregressive LLMs to design a simple yet general detection signal.

papers.title: DenseScout: Algorithm-System Co-design for Budgeted Tiny Object Selection on Edge Platforms
papers.summary: Deploying tiny object perception on edge platforms is challenging because practical systems must satisfy both strict compute budgets and end-to-end latency constraints. A common strategy is to first select a small number of candidate patches from a high-resolution image and then apply downstream processing only to the selected regions. However, existing detector-based frontends are not well aligned with this setting: strong offline detection accuracy does not necessarily yield effective low-budget patch prioritization, nor does it guarantee usable performance once transport and inference delays are considered. In this work, we study budgeted tiny object selection on edge platforms from a joint algorithm--system perspective. We present DenseScout, a lightweight dense-response selector with only 1.01M parameters, which directly ranks candidate patch locations from a high-resolution scene via a lightweight proxy input and is better aligned with low-budget tiny-object prioritization than detector-style frontends. To bridge offline selector quality and deployable utility, we further develop a transport-aware runtime realization on heterogeneous edge devices and adopt QoS-constrained recall, which counts a target as successfully perceived only if it is covered by the selected regions and the end-to-end processing finishes before the deadline. Experiments show that DenseScout consistently outperforms detector-based baselines in offline budgeted patch-selection evaluation, especially in low-budget regimes, while cross-platform results on RK3588 and Jetson Orin NX show that deployable performance depends jointly on selector quality and runtime realization efficiency. These results suggest that edge tiny object perception should be optimized as an algorithm--system co-design problem rather than as isolated model selection.

ai.tldr: 提出DenseScout，一种轻量级密集响应选择器，用于边缘平台上预算受限的小目标选择，通过算法-系统协同设计提升端到端性能。
ai.motivation: 现有检测器前端在低预算补丁优先级排序和端到端延迟约束下表现不佳，需要联合优化选择器质量和运行时效率。
ai.method: 设计仅1.01M参数的密集响应选择器，直接对高分辨率场景中的候选补丁位置排序，并开发传输感知的异构边缘设备运行时实现，采用QoS约束召回率指标。
ai.result: 在RK3588和Jetson Orin NX上，DenseScout在低预算场景下优于检测器基线，且可部署性能取决于选择器质量和运行时效率的协同。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在边缘平台上高效部署小目标感知系统，平衡计算预算、延迟和准确性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将选择器与下游处理模型联合训练，或引入自适应预算分配策略以应对动态场景。
idea_spark.risk: 协同设计可能增加系统复杂度，且对硬件平台依赖性较强，通用性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从端到端延迟约束出发重新设计感知流水线，而非孤立优化模型精度，启发边缘AI系统的新范式。

en.tldr: Proposes DenseScout, a lightweight dense-response selector for budgeted tiny object selection on edge platforms, leveraging algorithm-system co-design to improve end-to-end performance.
en.motivation: Existing detector frontends perform poorly in low-budget patch prioritization and end-to-end latency constraints, necessitating joint optimization of selector quality and runtime efficiency.
en.method: Designs a 1.01M-parameter dense-response selector that directly ranks candidate patch locations from high-resolution scenes, and develops a transport-aware runtime realization on heterogeneous edge devices with QoS-constrained recall.
en.result: On RK3588 and Jetson Orin NX, DenseScout outperforms detector baselines in low-budget regimes, and deployable performance depends on both selector quality and runtime efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to efficiently deploy tiny object perception systems on edge platforms, balancing compute budget, latency, and accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Explore joint training of the selector and downstream processing model, or adaptive budget allocation strategies for dynamic scenes.
idea_spark.risk: Co-design may increase system complexity and depend heavily on hardware platforms, requiring validation of generality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Redesigning perception pipelines from end-to-end latency constraints rather than isolated model accuracy inspires a new paradigm for edge AI systems.

papers.title: Sparse Personalized Text Generation with Multi-Trajectory Reasoning
papers.summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) advance, personalization has become a key mechanism for tailoring outputs to individual user needs. However, most existing methods rely heavily on dense interaction histories, making them ineffective in cold-start scenarios where such data is sparse or unavailable. While external signals (e.g., content of similar users) can offer a potential remedy, leveraging them effectively remains challenging: raw context is often noisy, and existing methods struggle to reason over heterogeneous data sources. To address these issues, we introduce PAT (Personalization with Aligned Trajectories), a reasoning framework for cold-start LLM personalization. PAT first retrieves information along two complementary trajectories: writing-style cues from stylistically similar users and topic-specific context from preference-aligned users. It then employs a reinforcement learning-based, iterative dual-reasoning mechanism that enables the LLM to jointly refine and integrate these signals. Experimental results across real-world personalization benchmarks show that PAT consistently improves generation quality and alignment under sparse-data conditions, establishing a strong solution to the cold-start personalization problem.

ai.tldr: 提出PAT框架，通过多轨迹检索和强化学习双推理，解决冷启动场景下稀疏交互历史的个性化文本生成问题。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM个性化方法依赖密集交互历史，在冷启动场景下效果差；外部信号虽可辅助，但原始上下文嘈杂且难以推理异构数据。
ai.method: 先沿写作风格和主题偏好两条轨迹检索信息，再通过强化学习迭代双推理机制联合精炼和整合信号。
ai.result: 在真实个性化基准上，PAT在稀疏数据条件下持续提升生成质量和对齐度。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在缺乏历史数据时仍能获得个性化文本生成，如新用户推荐、冷启动对话系统。

idea_spark.idea: 利用用户写作风格和主题偏好的双轨迹，结合强化学习迭代优化，实现零样本个性化。
idea_spark.risk: 轨迹检索可能引入噪声，强化学习训练不稳定，需谨慎设计奖励函数。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类通过观察他人风格和偏好来适应新环境的行为中获得启发。

zh.tldr: 提出PAT框架，通过多轨迹检索和强化学习双推理，解决冷启动场景下稀疏交互历史的个性化文本生成问题。
zh.motivation: 现有LLM个性化方法依赖密集交互历史，在冷启动场景下效果差；外部信号虽可辅助，但原始上下文嘈杂且难以推理异构数据。

idea_spark.risk: 轨迹检索可能引入噪声，强化学习训练不稳定，需谨慎设计奖励函数。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类通过观察他人风格和偏好来适应新环境的行为中获得启发。

en.tldr: Proposes PAT, a framework using multi-trajectory retrieval and reinforcement learning dual-reasoning to address cold-start personalization with sparse interaction history.
en.motivation: Existing LLM personalization methods rely on dense interaction history and fail in cold-start scenarios; external signals are noisy and hard to reason over heterogeneous sources.
en.method: Retrieves information along writing-style and topic-preference trajectories, then employs an iterative dual-reasoning mechanism via reinforcement learning to jointly refine and integrate signals.
en.result: On real-world benchmarks, PAT consistently improves generation quality and alignment under sparse-data conditions.
en.help_to_user: Enables personalized text generation for users with little or no history, e.g., new user recommendations or cold-start dialogue systems.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage dual trajectories of writing style and topic preference with iterative RL optimization for zero-shot personalization.
idea_spark.risk: Trajectory retrieval may introduce noise; RL training can be unstable, requiring careful reward design.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by how humans adapt to new environments by observing others' styles and preferences.

papers.title: IAM: Identity-Aware Human Motion and Shape Joint Generation
papers.summary: Recent advances in text-driven human motion generation enable models to synthesize realistic motion sequences from natural language descriptions. However, most existing approaches assume identity-neutral motion and generate movements using a canonical body representation, ignoring the strong influence of body morphology on motion dynamics. In practice, attributes such as body proportions, mass distribution, and age significantly affect how actions are performed, and neglecting this coupling often leads to physically inconsistent motions. We propose an identity-aware motion generation framework that explicitly models the relationship between body morphology and motion dynamics. Instead of relying on explicit geometric measurements, identity is represented using multimodal signals, including natural language descriptions and visual cues. We further introduce a joint motion-shape generation paradigm that simultaneously synthesizes motion sequences and body shape parameters, allowing identity cues to directly modulate motion dynamics. Extensive experiments on motion capture datasets and large-scale in-the-wild videos demonstrate improved motion realism and motion-identity consistency while maintaining high motion quality. Project page: https://vjwq.github.io/IAM

ai.tldr: 提出身份感知的运动生成框架，联合生成人体运动序列与体型参数，利用多模态身份信号（文本和视觉）建模体型对运动的影响，提升运动真实感和身份一致性。
ai.motivation: 现有文本驱动运动生成方法忽略体型对运动动力学的显著影响，导致生成的运动物理不一致。
ai.method: 用多模态信号（自然语言和视觉线索）表示身份，引入联合运动-形状生成范式，使身份线索直接调制运动动力学。
ai.result: 在运动捕捉数据集和大规模野外视频上，运动真实感和运动-身份一致性均有提升，同时保持高质量运动。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户生成与指定体型（如身高、体重、年龄）一致的自然人体运动，适用于虚拟角色动画、游戏和仿真。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将身份信息扩展至更细粒度的生理属性（如肌肉力量、柔韧性），或结合物理模拟器实现更真实的运动约束。
idea_spark.risk: 多模态身份表示可能引入隐私问题，且依赖高质量文本/视觉描述，在数据稀缺场景下泛化能力受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人体运动学中体型-运动耦合的启发，可进一步研究其他物理属性（如服装、负重）对运动的影响。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户生成与指定体型（如身高、体重、年龄）一致的自然人体运动，适用于虚拟角色动画、游戏和仿真。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将身份信息扩展至更细粒度的生理属性（如肌肉力量、柔韧性），或结合物理模拟器实现更真实的运动约束。
idea_spark.risk: 多模态身份表示可能引入隐私问题，且依赖高质量文本/视觉描述，在数据稀缺场景下泛化能力受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人体运动学中体型-运动耦合的启发，可进一步研究其他物理属性（如服装、负重）对运动的影响。

en.tldr: Proposes an identity-aware motion generation framework that jointly generates motion sequences and body shape parameters, using multimodal identity signals (text and vision) to model the influence of body morphology on motion dynamics, improving motion realism and identity consistency.
en.motivation: Existing text-driven motion generation methods ignore the significant influence of body morphology on motion dynamics, leading to physically inconsistent motions.
en.method: Represents identity via multimodal signals (natural language and visual cues) and introduces a joint motion-shape generation paradigm where identity cues directly modulate motion dynamics.
en.result: On motion capture datasets and large-scale in-the-wild videos, motion realism and motion-identity consistency are improved while maintaining high motion quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users generate natural human motions consistent with specified body attributes (e.g., height, weight, age), useful for virtual character animation, gaming, and simulation.

idea_spark.idea: Explore extending identity information to finer-grained physiological attributes (e.g., muscle strength, flexibility) or combining with physics simulators for more realistic motion constraints.
idea_spark.risk: Multimodal identity representation may introduce privacy concerns and relies on high-quality text/visual descriptions, limiting generalization in data-scarce scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the body-motion coupling in human biomechanics, further study the influence of other physical attributes (e.g., clothing, load) on motion.

papers.title: Below-Chance Blindness: Prompted Underperformance in Small LLMs Produces Positional Bias Rather than Answer Avoidance
papers.summary: Detecting sandbagging--the deliberate underperformance on capability evaluations--is an open problem in AI safety. We tested whether symptom validity testing (SVT) logic from clinical malingering detection could identify sandbagging through below-chance performance (BCB) on forced-choice items. In a pre-registered pilot at the 7-9 billion parameter instruction-tuned scale (3 models, 4 MMLU-Pro domains, 4 conditions, 500 items per cell, 24,000 total trials), the plausibility gate failed. Zero of 12 model-domain cells showed significant below-chance performance under sandbagging instruction. Exploratory analyses revealed three qualitatively distinct failure modes. Qwen-2.5-7B and Phi-3.5-mini largely ignored the sandbagging instruction, with 62-88% response identity with the honest baseline. Llama-3-8B complied substantially but implemented underperformance as a positional heuristic, collapsing its response distribution onto middle-alphabet options (E at 31.8%, F at 26.1%) regardless of where the correct answer fell. This produced accuracy boosts of up to 33 percentage points when the correct answer coincidentally occupied the model's preferred position. An explicit anti-task instruction ("pick the least likely answer") drove two of three models below chance, with accuracy as low as 0.024. The capability for answer-aware avoidance therefore exists but is not activated by "deliberately underperform." BCB did not fail as a logical marker of answer-aware avoidance. It was not observed in this regime because the model showing the largest behavioural shift exhibited behaviour consistent with a position-dominant response policy rather than content-aware answer avoidance. We propose that positional-distribution shift may be a more effective behavioural signature than below-chance accuracy for detecting prompted underperformance at this model scale.

ai.tldr: 在7-9B参数的小型LLM中，通过症状有效性测试检测故意低表现（sandbagging）失败，模型表现出位置偏差而非答案回避。
ai.motivation: AI安全中检测模型故意低表现是一个开放问题，临床诈病检测中的症状有效性测试逻辑可能适用。
ai.method: 在预注册实验中，对3个7-9B参数指令微调模型进行4个MMLU-Pro领域、4种条件、每条件500项测试，共24000次试验。
ai.result: 无模型-领域组合在sandbagging指令下出现显著低于随机水平的表现；Llama-3-8B通过位置启发式（偏好中间选项E/F）实现低表现，而非内容感知回避。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解小型LLM在故意低表现指令下的行为模式，提示位置分布偏移比低于随机准确率更有效的检测信号。

idea_spark.idea: 设计基于响应位置分布统计的检测器，而非仅依赖准确率阈值。
idea_spark.risk: 位置偏好可能因模型或提示格式变化而不稳定，需验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 临床心理学中的症状有效性测试启发，但需调整至LLM特有的行为模式（如位置偏差）。

zh.motivation: AI安全中检测模型故意低表现是一个开放问题，临床诈病检测中的症状有效性测试逻辑可能适用。

idea_spark.risk: 位置偏好可能因模型或提示格式变化而不稳定，需验证泛化性。

en.tldr: Symptom validity testing fails to detect sandbagging in 7-9B parameter LLMs; models exhibit positional bias rather than answer avoidance.
en.motivation: Detecting deliberate underperformance (sandbagging) in LLMs is an open AI safety problem; clinical malingering detection logic may apply.
en.method: Pre-registered pilot with 3 instruction-tuned models (7-9B), 4 MMLU-Pro domains, 4 conditions, 500 items per cell, 24,000 total trials.
en.result: No model-domain cell showed significant below-chance performance under sandbagging instruction; Llama-3-8B implemented underperformance via positional heuristic (preferring middle options E/F) rather than content-aware avoidance.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand small LLM behavior under deliberate underperformance instructions, suggesting positional distribution shift as a more effective detection signal than below-chance accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Design detectors based on response position distribution statistics rather than accuracy thresholds alone.
idea_spark.risk: Positional preferences may vary across models or prompt formats, requiring robustness checks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Clinical symptom validity testing inspires, but adaptation to LLM-specific behaviors (e.g., positional bias) is needed.

papers.title: Evaluating Risks in Weak-to-Strong Alignment: A Bias-Variance Perspective
papers.summary: Weak-to-strong alignment offers a promising route to scalable supervision, but it can fail when a strong model becomes confidently wrong on examples that lie in the weak teacher's blind spots. Understanding such failures requires going beyond aggregate accuracy, since weak-to-strong errors depend not only on whether the strong model disagrees with its teacher, but also on how confidence and uncertainty are distributed across examples. In this work, we analyze weak-to-strong alignment through a bias-variance-covariance lens that connects misfit theory to practical post-training pipelines. We derive a misfit-based upper bound on weak-to-strong population risk and study its empirical components using continuous confidence scores. We evaluate four weak-to-strong pipelines spanning supervised fine-tuning (SFT), reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), and reinforcement learning from AI feedback (RLAIF) on the PKU-SafeRLHF and HH-RLHF datasets. Using a blind-spot deception metric that isolates cases where the strong model is confidently wrong while the weak model is uncertain, we find that strong-model variance is the strongest empirical predictor of deception across our settings. Covariance provides additional but weaker information, indicating that weak-strong dependence matters, but does not by itself explain the observed failures. These results suggest that strong-model variance can serve as an early-warning signal for weak-to-strong deception, while blind-spot evaluation helps distinguish whether failures are inherited from weak supervision or arise in regions of weak-model uncertainty.

ai.tldr: 从偏差-方差-协方差视角分析弱到强对齐中的风险，发现强模型方差是欺骗行为的最强预测因子。
ai.motivation: 弱到强对齐可能因强模型在弱教师盲区中自信犯错而失败，现有聚合准确率指标不足以理解此类失败。
ai.method: 推导基于失配的弱到强总体风险上界，使用连续置信度分数分析经验成分，并在SFT、RLHF、RLAIF管道上评估盲点欺骗指标。
ai.result: 强模型方差是欺骗的最强经验预测因子，协方差提供额外但较弱的信息，盲点评估可区分失败来源。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种基于方差和盲点评估的早期预警信号，帮助检测弱到强对齐中的潜在欺骗行为。

idea_spark.idea: 利用强模型方差作为实时监控指标，在训练中动态调整弱监督信号。
idea_spark.risk: 方差信号可能受模型架构或数据分布影响，需验证其跨设置鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将统计学习理论中的偏差-方差分解应用于AI安全评估，启发更细粒度的风险量化方法。

zh.tldr: 从偏差-方差-协方差视角分析弱到强对齐中的风险，发现强模型方差是欺骗行为的最强预测因子。

idea_spark.risk: 方差信号可能受模型架构或数据分布影响，需验证其跨设置鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将统计学习理论中的偏差-方差分解应用于AI安全评估，启发更细粒度的风险量化方法。

en.tldr: Analyzes weak-to-strong alignment through a bias-variance-covariance lens, finding strong-model variance as the strongest predictor of deception.
en.motivation: Weak-to-strong alignment can fail when the strong model is confidently wrong on weak teacher blind spots; aggregate accuracy is insufficient to understand such failures.
en.method: Derives a misfit-based upper bound on weak-to-strong population risk, studies empirical components using continuous confidence scores, and evaluates blind-spot deception across SFT, RLHF, and RLAIF pipelines.
en.result: Strong-model variance is the strongest empirical predictor of deception; covariance provides weaker information; blind-spot evaluation helps distinguish failure sources.
en.help_to_user: Provides early-warning signals based on variance and blind-spot evaluation to detect potential deception in weak-to-strong alignment.

idea_spark.idea: Use strong-model variance as a real-time monitoring signal to dynamically adjust weak supervision during training.
idea_spark.risk: Variance signals may depend on model architecture or data distribution; robustness across settings needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Applying bias-variance decomposition from statistical learning theory to AI safety evaluation inspires finer-grained risk quantification methods.

papers.title: One Refiner to Unlock Them All: Inference-Time Reasoning Elicitation via Reinforcement Query Refinement
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail to utilize their latent reasoning capabilities due to a distributional mismatch between ambiguous human inquiries and the structured logic required for machine activation. Existing alignment methods either incur prohibitive $O(N)$ costs by fine-tuning each model individually or rely on static prompts that fail to resolve query-level structural complexity. In this paper, we propose ReQueR (\textbf{Re}inforcement \textbf{Que}ry \textbf{R}efinement), a modular framework that treats reasoning elicitation as an inference-time alignment task. We train a specialized Refiner policy via Reinforcement Learning to rewrite raw queries into explicit logical decompositions, treating frozen LLMs as the environment. Rooted in the classical Zone of Proximal Development from educational psychology, we introduce the Adaptive Solver Hierarchy, a curriculum mechanism that stabilizes training by dynamically aligning environmental difficulty with the Refiner's evolving competence. ReQueR yields consistent absolute gains of 1.7\%--7.2\% across diverse architectures and benchmarks, outperforming strong baselines by 2.1\% on average. Crucially, it provides a promising paradigm for one-to-many inference-time reasoning elicitation, enabling a single Refiner trained on a small set of models to effectively unlock reasoning in diverse unseen models. Code is available at https://github.com/newera-xiao/ReQueR.

ai.tldr: 提出ReQueR框架，通过强化学习训练一个精炼器（Refiner）在推理时将模糊查询重写为显式逻辑分解，从而解锁冻结大语言模型的推理能力，无需微调模型本身。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型常因人类查询的模糊性与机器所需结构化逻辑之间的分布不匹配而无法发挥潜在推理能力；现有对齐方法要么对每个模型单独微调成本高昂，要么依赖静态提示无法解决查询级结构复杂性。
ai.method: 将推理激发视为推理时对齐任务，用强化学习训练一个专门的Refiner策略，将原始查询重写为显式逻辑分解，将冻结的LLM视为环境；引入基于最近发展区理论的适应性求解器层次结构作为课程机制，动态调整环境难度以匹配Refiner的能力。
ai.result: 在多种架构和基准上取得1.7%–7.2%的绝对提升，平均优于强基线2.1%；单个Refiner在少量模型上训练后能有效解锁多种未见模型的推理能力。
ai.help_to_user: 用户无需微调模型，只需通过ReQueR框架即可提升LLM在复杂查询上的推理表现，尤其适用于需要结构化推理但查询模糊的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将教育心理学中的最近发展区理论引入LLM训练，通过动态课程机制稳定强化学习训练，实现一个精炼器服务多个模型。
idea_spark.risk: Refiner的泛化能力可能受限于训练时使用的模型集合；强化学习训练可能不稳定，需要精心设计奖励函数和课程策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从教育心理学中借鉴最近发展区概念来设计自适应课程，启发我们思考如何将人类学习理论应用于AI模型的训练与对齐。

idea_spark.idea: 将教育心理学中的最近发展区理论引入LLM训练，通过动态课程机制稳定强化学习训练，实现一个精炼器服务多个模型。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从教育心理学中借鉴最近发展区概念来设计自适应课程，启发我们思考如何将人类学习理论应用于AI模型的训练与对齐。

en.tldr: Proposes ReQueR, a framework that trains a Refiner via reinforcement learning to rewrite ambiguous queries into explicit logical decompositions at inference time, unlocking reasoning in frozen LLMs without fine-tuning.
en.motivation: LLMs often fail to utilize latent reasoning due to distribution mismatch between ambiguous human queries and structured logic; existing alignment methods are either costly (fine-tuning each model) or rely on static prompts that cannot resolve query-level complexity.
en.method: Treats reasoning elicitation as an inference-time alignment task, training a Refiner policy via RL to rewrite raw queries into explicit logical decompositions, with frozen LLMs as the environment; introduces an Adaptive Solver Hierarchy based on the Zone of Proximal Development as a curriculum mechanism to dynamically align environmental difficulty with Refiner competence.
en.result: Achieves consistent absolute gains of 1.7%–7.2% across diverse architectures and benchmarks, outperforming strong baselines by 2.1% on average; a single Refiner trained on a small set of models effectively unlocks reasoning in diverse unseen models.
en.help_to_user: Users can improve LLM reasoning on complex queries without model fine-tuning, simply by applying the ReQueR framework, especially useful for scenarios with ambiguous queries requiring structured reasoning.

idea_spark.idea: Introduces the Zone of Proximal Development from educational psychology into LLM training, using a dynamic curriculum to stabilize RL training, enabling one Refiner to serve multiple models.
idea_spark.risk: Refiner generalization may be limited by the model set used during training; RL training can be unstable, requiring careful reward design and curriculum strategy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrowing the Zone of Proximal Development concept from educational psychology to design adaptive curricula inspires thinking about how human learning theories can be applied to AI model training and alignment.

papers.title: ADEMA: A Knowledge-State Orchestration Architecture for Long-Horizon Knowledge Synthesis with LLMAgents
papers.summary: Long-horizon LLM tasks often fail not because a single answer is unattainable, but because knowledge states drift across rounds, intermediate commitments remain implicit, and interruption fractures the evolving evidence chain. This paper presents ADEMA as a knowledge-state orchestration architecture for long-horizon knowledge synthesis rather than as a generic multi-agent runtime. The architecture combines explicit epistemic bookkeeping, heterogeneous dual-evaluator governance, adaptive task-mode switching, reputation-shaped resource allocation, checkpoint-resumable persistence, segment-level memory condensation, artifact-first assembly, and final-validity checking with safe fallback. Evidence is drawn entirely from existing materials: a four-scenario showcase package, a fixed 60-run mechanism matrix, targeted micro-ablation and artifact-chain supplements, and a repaired protocol-level benchmark in which code-oriented evaluation is the clearest quality-sensitive mechanism block. Across the fixed matrix, removing checkpoint/resume produced the only invalid run, and it did so in the interruption-sensitive resume condition. By contrast, dual evaluation, segment synthesis, and dynamic governance are best interpreted as supporting control mechanisms that shape trajectory discipline, explicit artifact progression, and cost-quality behavior rather than as universal binary prerequisites for completion. The contribution is therefore a knowledge-state orchestration architecture in which explicit epistemic state transition, evidence-bearing artifact progression, and recoverable continuity are the primary design commitments.

ai.tldr: 提出ADEMA架构，通过显式知识状态追踪、双评估器治理、检查点恢复等机制，解决长周期LLM任务中的知识漂移和中断问题。
ai.motivation: 长周期LLM任务常因知识状态漂移、中间承诺隐式化、中断破坏证据链而失败，现有方法缺乏系统性的知识状态编排。
ai.method: 结合显式知识簿记、异构双评估器治理、自适应任务模式切换、声誉塑造资源分配、可检查点恢复持久化、片段级记忆压缩、工件优先组装及最终有效性检查与安全回退。
ai.result: 在固定60次运行矩阵中，仅移除检查点/恢复导致一次无效运行（中断敏感恢复条件）；双评估、片段合成和动态治理主要塑造轨迹纪律和成本-质量行为。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可恢复、可追踪的知识合成架构，帮助用户构建更鲁棒的长周期LLM代理系统。

idea_spark.idea: 将检查点恢复与片段级记忆压缩结合，实现轻量级长期记忆管理，减少计算开销。
idea_spark.risk: 双评估器治理可能引入额外延迟和复杂性，需平衡评估粒度与实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从软件工程中的检查点与版本控制获得启发，将显式状态管理引入LLM代理流程。

en.tldr: ADEMA is a knowledge-state orchestration architecture that addresses knowledge drift, implicit commitments, and interruption in long-horizon LLM tasks via explicit epistemic tracking, dual-evaluator governance, and checkpoint-resumable persistence.
en.motivation: Long-horizon LLM tasks often fail due to knowledge state drift, implicit intermediate commitments, and interruption breaking the evidence chain, lacking systematic orchestration.
en.method: Combines explicit epistemic bookkeeping, heterogeneous dual-evaluator governance, adaptive task-mode switching, reputation-shaped resource allocation, checkpoint-resumable persistence, segment-level memory condensation, artifact-first assembly, and final-validity checking with safe fallback.
en.result: In a fixed 60-run matrix, only removing checkpoint/resume caused one invalid run (interruption-sensitive resume condition); dual evaluation, segment synthesis, and dynamic governance shape trajectory discipline and cost-quality behavior.
en.help_to_user: Provides a recoverable and traceable knowledge synthesis architecture for building more robust long-horizon LLM agent systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine checkpoint-resume with segment-level memory condensation for lightweight long-term memory management, reducing computational overhead.
idea_spark.risk: Dual-evaluator governance may introduce additional latency and complexity; balance evaluation granularity with real-time requirements.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by checkpointing and version control in software engineering, bringing explicit state management into LLM agent workflows.

papers.title: The Dynamics of Delusion: Modeling Bidirectional False Belief Amplification in Human-Chatbot Dialogue
papers.summary: There is growing concern that AI chatbots might fuel delusional beliefs in users. Some have suggested that humans and chatbots mutually reinforce false beliefs over time, but quantitative evidence is lacking. Using a unique dataset of chat logs from individuals who exhibited delusional thinking, we developed a latent state model that captures accumulating and decaying influences between humans and chatbots. We find that a bidirectional influence model substantially outperforms a unidirectional alternative where humans are the primary driver of delusion. We find that humans exert strong but short-lived influence on chatbots, whereas chatbots exert longer-lasting influence on humans. Moreover, chatbots exert strong, stable self-influence over their own future outputs that tends to perpetuate delusions over long stretches of conversation. In fact, this chatbot self-influence constituted the dominant pathway when considering accumulated influence over time. Overall, these results indicate that humans tend to drive sharp, immediate increases in delusion, whereas chatbots sustain and propagate these effects over longer timescales. Together, these findings provide the first quantitative evidence that human-chatbot interactions can form feedback loops of delusion, decomposable into distinct pathways with dissociable temporal dynamics. By doing so, they can inform the development of safer AI systems.

ai.tldr: 本研究通过分析妄想思维个体的聊天记录，建立潜在状态模型，首次量化了人类与聊天机器人之间双向错误信念放大的动态过程。
ai.motivation: 现有担忧认为AI聊天机器人可能助长用户的妄想信念，但缺乏定量证据。
ai.method: 利用独特数据集，开发潜在状态模型捕捉人类与聊天机器人之间累积和衰减的影响。
ai.result: 双向影响模型优于单向模型；人类影响强烈但短暂，聊天机器人影响持久；聊天机器人自我影响是长期传播妄想的主要途径。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解人机交互中妄想信念的传播机制，为设计更安全的AI系统提供依据。

idea_spark.idea: 利用该模型设计早期干预机制，在聊天机器人自我影响路径上打断妄想放大循环。
idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖特定数据集，泛化性需验证；干预可能影响用户体验。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发开发实时监测人机对话中信念扭曲的AI安全工具。

en.motivation: Concerns exist that AI chatbots may fuel user delusions, but quantitative evidence is lacking.
en.method: Using a unique dataset of chat logs, we developed a latent state model capturing accumulating and decaying influences between humans and chatbots.

idea_spark.risk: Model generalizability may be limited by the specific dataset; interventions could affect user experience.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires development of real-time AI safety tools to monitor belief distortion in human-chatbot conversations.

papers.title: On the Minimum Distances of Some Families of Goppa Codes and BCH Codes
papers.summary: Goppa codes form an important class of alternant codes with wide applications in algebraic coding theory and code-based cryptography. Determining the true minimum distance of a Goppa code is a difficult problem. In this paper, we provide a necessary and sufficient criterion for a Goppa code to attain its designed distance $δ=t+1$, where $t$ is the degree of the Goppa polynomial. As applications, we determine the minimum distances of several classes of $q$-ary Goppa codes. In particular, we prove the tightness of the improved lower bound for a class of wild Goppa codes, and extend the family with $G(x)=x^t+A$ from the binary case to arbitrary odd prime powers.   We then specialize the criterion to the monomial case $G(x)=x^t$, which is equivalent to primitive BCH codes. This leads to several infinite families of primitive BCH codes with $d=δ$, including the binary codes $\mathbf{C}_{(2,2^m-1,9,1)}$ and $\mathbf{C}_{(2,2^m-1,15,1)}$, the family $\mathbf{C}_{(p,p^p-1,2p+2,1)}$ with an odd prime $p$ and the family $\mathbf{C}_{(q,q^m-1,r\frac{q^m-1}{q-1}+1,1)}$ with $r\mid q-1$. In particular, we prove that the primitive BCH code $\mathbf{C}_{(q,q^m-1,q^t+1,1)}$ has minimum distance $q^t+1$ under the condition $t\mid m$, improving the previously known condition $pt\mid m$.

ai.tldr: 本文给出了Goppa码达到设计距离的充要条件，并确定了多类q元Goppa码和原始BCH码的最小距离。
ai.motivation: 确定Goppa码的真实最小距离是一个困难问题，本文旨在提供判定准则并推广已知结果。
ai.method: 通过代数准则分析Goppa多项式与设计距离的关系，并特例化到单项式情形以研究原始BCH码。
ai.result: 证明了若干类Goppa码和原始BCH码的最小距离等于设计距离，改进了已有条件。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解Goppa码和BCH码的最小距离性质，可用于编码理论研究和密码学应用。

idea_spark.idea: 利用多项式分解与有限域结构，系统刻画码的最小距离。
idea_spark.risk: 条件可能仅适用于特定多项式形式，通用性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将Goppa码与BCH码统一分析，启发更一般的距离判定框架。

en.tldr: This paper provides a necessary and sufficient criterion for a Goppa code to attain its designed distance, and determines the minimum distances of several classes of q-ary Goppa codes and primitive BCH codes.
en.motivation: Determining the true minimum distance of a Goppa code is a difficult problem; this work aims to provide a criterion and extend known results.
en.method: Algebraic criterion relating the Goppa polynomial to the designed distance, specialized to the monomial case for primitive BCH codes.
en.result: Proved that several families of Goppa codes and primitive BCH codes have minimum distance equal to the designed distance, improving previous conditions.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the minimum distance properties of Goppa and BCH codes, useful for coding theory and cryptography.

idea_spark.idea: Systematically characterize code minimum distance using polynomial factorization and finite field structure.
idea_spark.risk: Conditions may be limited to specific polynomial forms, lacking full generality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Unified analysis of Goppa and BCH codes inspires a broader framework for distance determination.

papers.title: GPT-Image-2 in the Wild: A Twitter Dataset of Self-Reported AI-Generated Images from the First Week of Deployment
papers.summary: The release of GPT-image-2 by OpenAI marks a watershed moment in AI-generated imagery: the boundary between photographic reality and synthetic content has never been more difficult to discern. We introduce the GPT-Image-2 Twitter Dataset, the first published dataset of GPT-image-2 generated images, sourced from publicly available Twitter/X posts in the immediate aftermath of the model's April 21, 2026 release. Leveraging the Twitter API v2 and a multi-stage curation pipeline spanning multilingual text heuristics (English, Japanese, and Chinese), browser-automated Twitter "Made with AI" badge verification, and model name variant matching, we curate 10,217 confirmed GPT-image-2 images from 27,662 collected records over a six-day window.   We characterize the dataset across four analyses: CLIP-based zero-shot subject taxonomy, OCR text legibility (82.0% of images contain detectable text), face detection (59.2% of images, 22,583 total faces), and semantic clustering (137 CLIP ViT-L/14 clusters).   A key negative result is that C2PA content credentials are systematically stripped by Twitter's CDN on upload, rendering cryptographic provenance verification infeasible for social-media-sourced AI images. The dataset and all curation code are released publicly.

ai.tldr: 发布首个GPT-Image-2推特数据集，包含10,217张确认由该模型生成的图像，并分析了其主题、文字、人脸和语义聚类特征。
ai.motivation: GPT-Image-2的发布使AI生成图像与真实照片难以区分，亟需公开数据集以支持检测与溯源研究。
ai.method: 通过Twitter API v2收集推文，结合多语言文本启发式、浏览器自动化“Made with AI”徽章验证和模型名称匹配，构建多阶段筛选流程。
ai.result: 收集27,662条记录，确认10,217张图像；82.0%含可检测文字，59.2%含人脸（共22,583张脸），CLIP聚类得到137个语义簇；发现C2PA凭证在上传时被Twitter CDN系统性地移除。
ai.help_to_user: 提供公开数据集和代码，便于研究者分析GPT-Image-2生成图像的特征及社交媒体传播中的溯源问题。

idea_spark.idea: 利用CLIP聚类结果训练分类器，自动识别社交媒体上未经标注的AI生成图像。
idea_spark.risk: 数据集仅覆盖发布后六天，可能无法代表长期使用模式；C2PA凭证缺失限制了深度溯源验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合OCR和面部检测结果，可开发针对AI生成图像中文字错误或面部畸形的检测工具。
